Items.Title: 使用 Athena 查询多账户 Cloudwatch 日志 | Using Athena to query multi-account Cloudwatch Logs
Items.Description:
情景。 我们将多个工作负载和环境部署到 AWS Organization 中的多账户组织。 为了增加对应用程序日志的理解，将来自不同 AWS 账户的日志聚合到单个服务或 S3 存储桶可能会有所帮助。 根据业务的不同，合规性也可能要求您将日志保留一定时间。 在实施访问控制和保留计划时，处理集中帐户中的长时间日志存储会有所帮助。

这篇文章演示了如何在 AWS Organizations 中对多账户组织实施集中式日志存储。 主要目标是创建云基础架构以：

  • 将来自多个 AWS 账户的应用程序日志存储到单个 S3 存储桶中。
  • 使用 Amazon Athena 对 S3 中的数据运行临时查询。

让我们开始吧！

概述

建议的解决方案使用 Cloudwatch Logs 来获取源帐户的日志。 最后，我们将使用 Amazon Athena 对这些日志运行 SQL 查询。

部署

可在此处找到日志记录解决方案的 Cloudformation 模板： https ://github.com/markymarkus/cloudformation/tree/master/centralised-cloudwatch-logs

模板参数OrganizationId用于Cloudwatch Logs 目标访问策略，以允许从 AWS Organizations 成员账户传输日志。

您也可以在部署logging-account-template.yml的同一帐户上创建它。

日志源帐户

源账户有工作负载产生和摄取日志到 Cloudwatch Logs。 每个日志组都存储来自特定源的日志记录事件。 在我们的示例中：
当我们对数据运行 Athena 查询时，为日志组提供一致的命名约定会很方便。 诸如“组织的每个 AWS 账户中每个 EC2 实例的 /var/log/messages”之类的查询依赖于一致的命名。

日志通过订阅过滤器从成员帐户发送到日志存储帐户中的接收集中目的地。

日志存储帐户

日志存储帐户为 Athena 接收和准备日志数据，并将日志存储到 S3 存储桶。 日志以每行格式存储在一个 JSON 记录中。 由 Athena 支持，易于导出其他服务和工具。

转换日志事件

默认情况下，Firehose 将流中的 JSON 记录写入 S3 存储桶，不带分隔符或换行符。 如果我们不使用 Athena 运行查询，这将起作用。 为了拆分 JSON 记录，我们使用Firehose 转换 Lambda 。 处理后的记录被写回 Firehose 流，最终将日志传送到 S3 存储桶。

 output = [] for record in event['records']: payload = base64.b64decode(record['data']) striodata = BytesIO(payload) with gzip.GzipFile(fileobj=striodata, mode='r') as f: payload = f.read().decode("utf-8") # Add newline to each record output_record = { 'recordId': record['recordId'], 'result': 'Ok', 'data': base64.b64encode((payload + "\n").encode("utf-8")) } output.append(output_record)

运行 Athena 查询

此时，我们在 Log Storage 帐户的 S3 存储桶中拥有应用程序日志。 要运行 Athena 查询，首先创建指向数据的外部表：

 CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE logs ( messageType string, owner string, logGroup string, subscriptionFilters string, logEvents array<struct<id:string, timestamp:string, message:string >> ) ROW FORMAT SERDE 'org.openx.data.jsonserde.JsonSerDe' LOCATION 's3://BUCKET_NAME/logs/year=2022/month=05/day=05/'

为了查询数组中的 Cloudwatch Logs 字段，您需要UNNEST logEvents。 这里有几个查询可以帮助您入门：

第一个查询从源帐户返回最新的流式日志：

 SELECT owner,loggroup,n.message FROM logs CROSS JOIN UNNEST(logs.logevents) AS t (n) LIMIT 20 ###### 1 111111111111 /var/log/messages May 5 03:07:48 ip-10-0-24-56 dhclient[2085]: XMT: Solicit on eth0, interval 119340ms. 2 222222222222 /aws/lambda/hello-world-lambda START RequestId: fcc9e873-d4c1-4ca3-a7de-fd5490300740 Version: $LATEST 3 222222222222 /aws/lambda/hello-world-lambda [DEBUG] 2022-05-05T03:07:50.972Z fcc9e873-d4c1-4ca3-a7de-fd5490300740 {'version': '0', 'id': .... 4 222222222222 /aws/lambda/hello-world-lambda [DEBUG] 2022-05-05T03:07:50.973Z fcc9e873-d4c1-4ca3-a7de-fd5490300740 Hello World! 5 111111111111 /aws/lambda/lambda-writer-LambdaFunction START RequestId: 38fb9b6b-dbea-4875-91cc-cf1dd5b36ab9 Version: $LATEST 6 111111111111 /aws/lambda/lambda-writer-LambdaFunction [DEBUG] 2022-05-05T03:08:16.81Z 38fb9b6b-dbea-4875-91cc-cf1dd5b36ab9 7 111111111111 /var/log/messages May 5 11:40:01 ip-10-0-24-56 systemd: Created slice User Slice of root. 8 111111111111 /var/log/messages May 5 11:40:01 ip-10-0-24-56 systemd: Started Session 169 of user root.

下一个 SQL 查询返回每个日志记录源（日志组）的日志事件计数：

 SELECT owner,loggroup,count(*) FROM logs CROSS JOIN UNNEST(logs.logevents) AS t (n) GROUP BY owner,loggroup ### 1 111111111111 /var/log/secure 429 2 111111111111 /var/log/messages 1670 3 222222222222 /aws/lambda/hello-world-lambda 5764 4 111111111111 /aws/lambda/lambda-writer-LambdaFunction 7198 ...

结论

如果您正在构建和规划日志记录策略，则此解决方案可能是一个很好的起点。 您可以将 Cloudwatch 日志从多个账户和区域收集到单个 S3 存储桶。 可以针对合并的日志记录数据执行 Athena 查询。 我鼓励您尝试对日志数据进行 SQL 查询。 分析源特定的日志记录模式和事件数量可以帮助您改进整体日志管理过程。

谢谢阅读。



原文:

The scenario. We have multiple workloads and environments deployed to multi-account organization in AWS Organization. Cloudwatch Logs is used to to store logs from various services within a scope of a single AWS account. EC2s are pushing system logs, Lambda functions pushing execution logs and so on.

In order to increase understanding on application logs, aggregating logs from separate AWS accounts to a single service or S3 bucket can be helpful. Depending on business, regulatory compliance may also oblige you to keep logs for certain amount of time. Handling long time log storage in a centralised account can help when implementing access control and retention schedules.

This post demonstrates how to implement centralised log storage to multi-account organization in AWS Organizations. Main goal is to create cloud infrastructure to:

  • Store application logs from multiple AWS accounts to a single S3 bucket.
  • Run ad-hoc queries agains data in S3 with Amazon Athena.

Let's get started!

Overview

The proposed solution uses Cloudwatch Logs for log ingestion on source accounts. Amazon Kinesis is used for log delivery to centralised storage and S3 for long term log data storage. Finally we will use Amazon Athena to run SQL queries against these logs.

Deployment

Cloudformation templates for the logging solution can be found here: https://github.com/markymarkus/cloudformation/tree/master/centralised-cloudwatch-logs

logging-account-template.yml provisions infrastructure for Log Storage Account. Template parameter OrganizationId is used on Cloudwatch Logs Destination access policy to allow log delivery from AWS Organizations member accounts.

member-account-template.yml demonstrates how to create Subscription Filter to Cloudwatch Logs. You can create this also on the same account where logging-account-template.yml is deployed.

Log Source Accounts

Source accounts have workloads producing and ingesting logs to Cloudwatch Logs. Each log group is storing logging events from a specific source. In our example:
/var/log/messages stores system logs from EC2 instances.
/aws/lambda/hello-world stores logs from Hello World Lambda function.
Having consistent naming conventions for log groups will come handy when we run Athena queries against the data. Queries like '/var/log/messages of every EC2 instance in every AWS account of Organization' are depended on consistent naming.

Logs are sent from a member account to a receiving centralised destination in Log Storage Account through a subscription filter.

Log Storage Account

Log Storage Account receives and prepares logging data for Athena and stores logs to S3 bucket. Logs are stored in one JSON record per line format. Supported by Athena and easy to export other services and tools.

Transforming log events

By default, Firehose writes JSON records in a stream to S3 bucket without separators or new lines. This would work if we would not be using Athena to run queries. Athena requires each JSON record to be represented in a separate line.
To split JSON records we are using Firehose transformation Lambda. Lambda function reads records batch and adds a new line character + "\n" after every record. Processed records are written back to Firehose stream which finally delivers logs to S3 bucket.

output = []
for record in event['records']:
    payload = base64.b64decode(record['data'])
    striodata = BytesIO(payload)
    with gzip.GzipFile(fileobj=striodata, mode='r') as f:
        payload = f.read().decode("utf-8")

    # Add newline to each record
    output_record = {
        'recordId': record['recordId'],
        'result': 'Ok',
        'data': base64.b64encode((payload + "\n").encode("utf-8"))
    }
    output.append(output_record)

Running Athena queries

At this point we have application logs in S3 bucket in Log Storage account. Kinesis is a time based stream so each file contains logging from multiple Source AWS accounts and log groups:

To get Athena queries running, first create external table pointing to the data:

CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE logs (
    messageType string,
    owner string,
    logGroup string,
    subscriptionFilters string,
    logEvents array<struct<id:string,
              timestamp:string,
              message:string
              >>
  )           
ROW FORMAT SERDE 'org.openx.data.jsonserde.JsonSerDe'
LOCATION 's3://BUCKET_NAME/logs/year=2022/month=05/day=05/'

logEvents are stored in array structure. In order to query Cloudwatch Logs fields within an array, you need to UNNEST logEvents. Here are couple of queries to get you started:

First query returns latest streamed logs from Source Accounts:

SELECT owner,loggroup,n.message FROM logs
CROSS JOIN UNNEST(logs.logevents) AS t (n)
LIMIT 20
######
1   111111111111    /var/log/messages   May 5 03:07:48 ip-10-0-24-56 dhclient[2085]: XMT: Solicit on eth0, interval 119340ms.
2   222222222222    /aws/lambda/hello-world-lambda  START RequestId: fcc9e873-d4c1-4ca3-a7de-fd5490300740 Version: $LATEST
3   222222222222    /aws/lambda/hello-world-lambda  [DEBUG] 2022-05-05T03:07:50.972Z fcc9e873-d4c1-4ca3-a7de-fd5490300740 {'version': '0', 'id': ....
4   222222222222    /aws/lambda/hello-world-lambda  [DEBUG] 2022-05-05T03:07:50.973Z fcc9e873-d4c1-4ca3-a7de-fd5490300740 Hello World!
5   111111111111    /aws/lambda/lambda-writer-LambdaFunction    START RequestId: 38fb9b6b-dbea-4875-91cc-cf1dd5b36ab9 Version: $LATEST
6   111111111111    /aws/lambda/lambda-writer-LambdaFunction    [DEBUG] 2022-05-05T03:08:16.81Z 38fb9b6b-dbea-4875-91cc-cf1dd5b36ab9
7   111111111111    /var/log/messages   May 5 11:40:01 ip-10-0-24-56 systemd: Created slice User Slice of root.
8   111111111111    /var/log/messages   May 5 11:40:01 ip-10-0-24-56 systemd: Started Session 169 of user root.

Next SQL query return log event count for each logging source(Log group):


SELECT owner,loggroup,count(*) FROM logs
CROSS JOIN UNNEST(logs.logevents) AS t (n)
GROUP BY owner,loggroup
###
1   111111111111    /var/log/secure 429
2   111111111111    /var/log/messages   1670
3   222222222222    /aws/lambda/hello-world-lambda  5764
4   111111111111    /aws/lambda/lambda-writer-LambdaFunction    7198
...

Conclusion

If you are in process of building and planning a logging strategy, this solution can be a good starting point. You can collect Cloudwatch Logs from multiple accounts and regions to a single S3 bucket. Athena queries can be executed against the consolidated logging data. I encourage you to experiment with SQL queries to the logging data. Analysing source specific logging patterns and event amounts may help you to improve an overall log management process.

Thanks for reading.
Items.Author: markymarkus

Items.Title: 开发录音师功能的重要提示 | Top Tips for Developing a Recordist Function
Items.Description:
杰克逊为HMS 核心

发表于高效的记录管理现在比以往任何时候都更加重要。 在我们的数字时代，信息的巨大增长——音频、视频等——必须在有限的时间内处理。 这使得实时转录功能必不可少，因为它在许多场景中都很有用。 在音频或视频会议中，这个功能可以记录会议纪要，我以后可以参考，比自己写要方便。 我看到我的孩子在他们的在线课程中努力记笔记，所以我知道在转录功能的帮助下这个过程可以变得更加容易。 简而言之，它取消了写下老师所说的所有内容的工作，让孩子们可以专注于讲座本身，并在以后轻松地复习内容。 此外，实时字幕为观众提供实时字幕，以获得更好的观看体验。

作为一名程序员，我坚信“行动胜于雄辩”。 这就是为什么我在 ML Kit 的实时转录功能的帮助下开发了一个实时转录功能，就像这样。

演示

此功能可将长达 5 小时的语音实时转录为中文、英文（或两者）和法文。 此外，输出文本带有标点符号并包含时间戳。 此功能有一些要求：法语支持取决于手机型号，而中英文在所有手机型号上都可用。 此外，该功能需要互联网连接。

好的，让我们继续本文的重点：我是如何开发这个实时转录功能的。

开发流程

一世。 做好必要的准备。 这在参考资料部分有详细描述。 创建并配置语音识别器。

 MLSpeechRealTimeTranscriptionConfig config = new MLSpeechRealTimeTranscriptionConfig.Factory() // Set the language, which can be Chinese, English, both Chinese and English, or French. .setLanguage(MLSpeechRealTimeTranscriptionConstants.LAN_ZH_CN) // Punctuate the text recognized from the speech. .enablePunctuation(true) // Set the sentence offset. .enableSentenceTimeOffset(true) // Set the word offset. .enableWordTimeOffset(true) .create(); MLSpeechRealTimeTranscription mSpeechRecognizer = MLSpeechRealTimeTranscription.getInstance();

为语音识别结果侦听器创建回调。

 // Use the callback to implement the [MLSpeechRealTimeTranscriptionListener](https://developer.huawei.com/consumer/en/doc/development/hiai-References/mlspeechrealtimetranscriptionlistener-0000001159518088) API and methods in the API. Protected class SpeechRecognitionListener implements MLSpeechRealTimeTranscriptionListener{ @Override public void onStartListening() { // The recorder starts to receive speech. } @Override public void onStartingOfSpeech() { // The speech recognizer detects the user speaking. } @Override public void onVoiceDataReceived(byte[] data, float energy, Bundle bundle) { // Return the original PCM stream and audio power to the user. The API does not run in the main thread, and the return result is processed in a sub-thread. } @Override public void onRecognizingResults(Bundle partialResults) { // Receive recognized text from **MLSpeechRealTimeTranscription**. } @Override public void onError(int error, String errorMessage) { // Callback when an error occurs during recognition. } @Override public void onState(int state,Bundle params) { // Notify the app of the recognizer status change. } }

绑定语音识别器。

 mSpeechRecognizer.setRealTimeTranscriptionListener(new SpeechRecognitionListener());

六。 识别完成后停止识别并释放识别器占用的资源。

 if (mSpeechRecognizer!= null) { mSpeechRecognizer.destroy(); }

参考

斯蒂芬妮 Repetcki - Apr 27 4月27日



原文:

Jackson for HMS Core

Posted on May 7

Efficient records management is more relevant now than ever. In our digital age, huge growth of information — audio, video, and more — must be handled in a limited time. This makes a real-time transcription function essential, because it is useful in many scenarios. In audio or video conferencing, this function records meeting minutes that I can refer to later, which is more convenient than writing them all by myself. I've seen my kids struggling to take notes during their online courses, so I know this process can be so much easier with the help of the transcription function. In short, it removed the job of writing down everything the teacher says, allowing the kids to focus on the lecture itself and easily review the content again later. Also, the live captions provide viewers with real-time subtitles, for a better watching experience.

As a coder, I am a believer in "Actions speak louder than words". That's why I developed a real-time transcription function, with the help of a real-time transcription capability from ML Kit, like this.

Demo

This function transcribes up to 5 hours of speech into Chinese, English (or both), and French languages in real time. In addition, the output text is punctuated and contains timestamps. This function has some requirements: the support for French is dependent on the mobile phone model, whereas Chinese and English are available on all mobile phone models. Also, the function requires Internet connection.

Okay, let's move on to the point of this article: How I developed this real-time transcription function.

Development Procedure

i. Make necessary preparations. This is described in detail in the References section. ii. Create and then configure a speech recognizer.

MLSpeechRealTimeTranscriptionConfig config = new MLSpeechRealTimeTranscriptionConfig.Factory()
    // Set the language, which can be Chinese, English, both Chinese and English, or French.
    .setLanguage(MLSpeechRealTimeTranscriptionConstants.LAN_ZH_CN)
    // Punctuate the text recognized from the speech.
    .enablePunctuation(true)
    // Set the sentence offset.
    .enableSentenceTimeOffset(true)
    // Set the word offset.
    .enableWordTimeOffset(true)
    .create();
MLSpeechRealTimeTranscription mSpeechRecognizer = MLSpeechRealTimeTranscription.getInstance();

iii. Create a callback for the speech recognition result listener.

// Use the callback to implement the [MLSpeechRealTimeTranscriptionListener](https://developer.huawei.com/consumer/en/doc/development/hiai-References/mlspeechrealtimetranscriptionlistener-0000001159518088) API and methods in the API.
Protected class SpeechRecognitionListener implements MLSpeechRealTimeTranscriptionListener{
    @Override
    public void onStartListening() {
        // The recorder starts to receive speech.
    }

    @Override
    public void onStartingOfSpeech() {
        // The speech recognizer detects the user speaking.
    }

    @Override
    public void onVoiceDataReceived(byte[] data, float energy, Bundle bundle) {
        // Return the original PCM stream and audio power to the user. The API does not run in the main thread, and the return result is processed in a sub-thread.
   }

    @Override
    public void onRecognizingResults(Bundle partialResults) {
        // Receive recognized text from **MLSpeechRealTimeTranscription**.
    }

    @Override
    public void onError(int error, String errorMessage) {
        // Callback when an error occurs during recognition.
    }

    @Override
    public void onState(int state,Bundle params) {
        // Notify the app of the recognizer status change.
    }
}

iv. Bind the speech recognizer.

mSpeechRecognizer.setRealTimeTranscriptionListener(new SpeechRecognitionListener());

v. Call startRecognizing to begin speech recognition.

mSpeechRecognizer.startRecognizing(config);

vi. Stop recognition and release resources occupied by the recognizer when the recognition is complete.

if (mSpeechRecognizer!= null) {
    mSpeechRecognizer.destroy();
}

References

Stefany Repetcki - Apr 27
Items.Author: jacksssson

Items.Title: 设置您的 Apple Watch 通知，让您的一天更加专注 | Set Up Your Apple Watch Notifications for a More Mindful Day
Items.Description:
当 Apple Watch 于 2015 年首次问世时，它迎来了智能可穿戴设备的新时代。 如今，Apple Watch 是一款出色的健身追踪器，并提供了许多健康追踪功能，如心率监测、睡眠和血氧水平追踪。 如果您丢失的 iPhone 滑入您沙发的一个缝隙中，它会使您丢失的 iPhone 响起来非常响亮。 它可以拨打和接听电话。 这使得使用 Apple Pay 支付非常方便。 简而言之，随着时间的推移，Apple Watch 已经慢慢开始成为具有多种用途的瑞士军刀。 但最重要的是，它会显示来自 iPhone 的应用通知，让您始终了解最新动态。

通知或不通知🤔

这实际上给我们带来了拥有 Apple Watch 的缺点之一。 您的所有通知都在 Apple Watch 上一键发送。 每次发送微不足道的推送通知时，您的手腕都会被轻拍。 手腕上的这种敲击需要您立即注意。 只需瞥一眼您的 Apple Watch 就可以了解它的全部内容，这会分散您的注意力并分散您当时正在处理的任务的注意力。 我们知道通知可能会带来很大的问题，因为它们会分散我们更专注的工作的注意力。 他们让我们去寻找每个通知可以给我们的那少量多巴胺。 根据一项研究，分心后，我们需要大约 30 分钟重新集中注意力。 在现代知识经济中，我们的注意力和时间是我们最宝贵的资产，通知对它没有任何贡献。

总而言之，通知会对我们的注意力产生不利影响，尤其是当它发生在我们的手腕上时。 我们可以简单地将手机放在另一个房间并完全忽略它。 但是，Apple Watch 是一种佩戴在手腕上并随身携带的设备。 是时候在您的 Apple Watch 上反击这些通知并收回我们应得的关注了。

关闭除必需品以外的所有通知🧘‍♀️


最好的办法是将这些通知留在您的 iPhone 上，并且不要在 Apple Watch 上显示其中的大部分。 在 iPhone 上打开Watch应用程序，然后单击通知部分。 您现在将看到两个应用程序列表。 第一个列表用于配置 Apple 原生应用程序的通知。 第二个列表适用于所有第三方应用程序。 我们的建议是关闭所有通知，除了非常重要的电话和聊天应用程序。

临时模式：使用焦点模式💆


如果关闭除基本信息以外的所有通知感觉太令人生畏，您可以选择一种不同的方法，使用 Apple Watch 上的一种对焦模式。 如果您知道在接下来的三个小时内您将非常专注地工作，您只需在 Apple Watch 上打开“请勿打扰”，以确保除了您的一位紧急联系人呼叫您时，不会收到任何通知。 此方法的缺点是它需要您手动打开和关闭此模式。

如果内容有帮助，请随时在这里支持我：



原文:

When the Apple Watch first came out in 2015, it ushered in a new age of smart wearables. Nowadays, the Apple Watch is an excellent fitness tracker and provides many health tracking features like heart-rate monitoring, sleep and blood-oxygen-level tracking. It can make your lost iPhone ring very loudly if it slipped in one of the crevices of your couch. It can make and receive phone calls. It makes paying with Apple Pay very convenient. Simply put, through time, the Apple Watch has slowly started te become a Swiss army knife for many purposes. But above all, it shows your app notifications from your iPhone so you're always up-to-date on the latest stories.

To be notified or not to be notified 🤔

This actually brings us to one of the down-sides of owning an Apple Watch. All your notifications are just one buzz away on your Apple Watch. Every time a push notification is sent for something trivial, your wrist will be tapped ever so gently. This tapping on your wrist is demanding your immediate attention. Just glancing on your Apple Watch to see what it was all about breaks your focus and distracts you from the task that you're working on at that very moment. We know that notifications can be very problematic since they serve as a big source of distractions from our more focused work. They condition us to seek out that small shot of dopamine each notification can give us. According to a study, we need around 30 minutes to re-focus our attention after a distraction. In the modern knowledge economy where our focus and time are our most valued assets, notifications don't contribute anything to it. 

To sum it all up, notifications have a detrimental effect on our focus, especially when it's happening on our wrist. We can simply put our phone in a different room and ignore it completely. However, an Apple Watch is a device that you wear on your wrist and carry with you everywhere. It's time to fight back those notifications on your Apple Watch and take back the focus that we so deserve.

Turn off all notifications except for the essentials 🧘‍♀️


Notifications for many apps on your Apple Watch are turned on by default and mirror what's happening on your iPhone. The best is to leave those notifications on your iPhone and don't show most of them on your Apple Watch. Open the Watch app on your iPhone and click on the Notifications section. You will now see two lists of apps. The first list is for configuring the notifications of Apple's native apps. The second list is for all the third-party apps. Our suggestion is to turn off all notifications except for the very essential - phone calls and chat apps.

Temporary mode: use focus modes 💆


If turning off all notifications except for the essentials feels too daunting, you can opt for a different approach with one of the focus modes on Apple Watch. If you know you will be working very focused for the next three hours, you can simply turn on Do Not Disturb on your Apple Watch to make sure that no notifications reach you except for when you're called by one of your emergency contacts. The down-side to this method is that it requires you to manually turn this mode on and off.

If the content was helpful, feel free to support me here:
Items.Author: duncanlew

Items.Title: Prowritingaid vs 语法 | Prowritingaid vs Grammarly
Items.Description:
我想讨论 ProwritingAid 和 Grammarly 之间的区别，这两个伟大的基于互联网的英语拼写检查器。 但是，这两种工具具有相似的功能，包括针对拼写、同义词和短语的大量单词建议列表。

这两个工具之间最显着的区别是 ProWritingAid 专注于提供同义词的快速查找和常见错误的详尽列表。 同时，Grammarly 更专注于改善您的写作风格并提供有关语法规则的额外帮助。 虽然 Pro WritingAid 比 Grammarly 有更多功能，例如语法生成器，但它似乎更适合已经熟悉英语的人，不需要花太多时间来修复常见错误。

什么是语法？
这是一个漂亮的小工具，具有大量功能，可帮助您提高语法技能。 一旦你掌握了使用它的窍门，你会想知道没有它你是如何尝试学习语法的。 但是，当然，它还与许多其他出色的在线程序捆绑在一起，这些程序将帮助您在舒适的家中尽可能多地学习。

它带有一个名为 Grammarly Free 的免费版本。 这个版本的作用本质上是整个应用程序的浓缩和简化版本，让您可以重温您在写作生涯中可能犯的那些基本语法和拼写错误。 这个免费版本非常好，尽管它有缺点：它确实允许你查看你的写作，看看它与包的其他部分相比如何，但它没有 Grammarly Premium 提供的任何高级功能，例如语法提示、校对工具或自动拼写检查。

什么是 ProWritingAid？
这很棒，因为它可以让您纠正拼写错误，而无需花时间去查字典或花费数小时重写。 使用这个方便的工具，您可以节省大量时间。 语法和拼写检查工具是任何想要写出成功论文的人的必备工具。 使用 ProwritingAid 工具，您可以享受高质量语法和拼写软件带来的所有好处。

该工具会自动识别与其他文档元素相似的所有文档元素，并为您提供一个标记，指示这些元素是否被复制或相似。

语法和 ProWritingAid 之间的区别
毫无疑问，ProwritingAid 和 Grammarly 都可以帮助您提高英语语法。 但是，这些程序在提高您的写作技巧方面存在显着差异。 因此，在决定应使用哪个程序来满足您的需求时，考虑这些程序之间的差异非常重要。 您还需要知道您是哪种作家，以及这两个程序如何最适合您。

它提供了多种风格、语法和词汇建议。 任何关于ProWritingAid 与 Grammarly的讨论都不完整。

根据我们的测试，Grammarly 对于非小说类写作是 100% 准确的。 甚至 Grammarly 的免费版本也提供了许多功能。 另一方面，免费版的 PWA 功能有限。 但是，ProWritingAid 可用于帮助您处理碎片化的句子、对话等。 它对小说作家也有帮助。 不幸的是，ProWritingAid 界面看起来不如 Grammarly。 此外，ProWritingAid 可以处理碎片化的句子。 语法成本高于 ProWritingAid。

这个语法检查工具也可以在 iOS 和 Android 手机上使用。 不幸的是，ProWritingAid (PWA) 不支持 iOS 或 Android 的移动应用程序。



原文:

I want to discuss the differences between ProwritingAid and Grammarly, two great internet-based English spelling checkers. However, both tools have similar features, including an extensive list of word suggestions for spellings, synonyms, and phrases.

The most significant difference between these two tools is that ProWritingAid focuses on providing a quick look-up of synonyms and an exhaustive list of common errors. At the same time, Grammarly is more focused on improving your writing style and providing extra help regarding grammar rules. Although Pro WritingAid has a few more features than Grammarly, such as a grammar generator, it seems to be geared more towards people familiar with English already and don’t need to take much time fixing common mistakes.

What is Grammarly?
It’s a beautiful little tool with tons of features that will help you become more advanced with your grammar skills. Once you get the hang of using it, you’ll wonder how you ever tried to learn grammar without it. But, of course, it also comes bundled with many of the other great online programs that will help you to learn as much as you can from the comfort of your own home.

It comes with a free version called Grammarly Free. What this version does is essentially a condensed and simplified version of the entire application, allowing you to brush up on those basic grammar and spelling mistakes that you may have made for your writing career. This free version is quite good, despite its shortcomings: it does allow you to take a look at your writing and see how it compares to the rest of the pack, but it doesn’t have any of the advanced features that Grammarly Premium offers, such as grammar hints, proofreading tools, or automatic spell-checking.

What is ProWritingAid?
ProwritingAid is an excellent grammar and spelling checker that also helps with writing. It’s great because it enables you to correct your spelling mistakes without taking time to go to the dictionary or spend hours rewriting. ProwritingAid Tool is an online spelling and grammar checking tool that works by showing you the proper rules for using words in sentences and helping you develop your writing skills.

ProwritingAid provides a great many benefits that will help you succeed with your writing. You can save a lot of time with this handy tool. Grammar and spelling checker tools are a must for anyone who wants to write successful papers. With the ProwritingAid Tool, you can enjoy all of the benefits that come with high-quality grammar and spelling software.

ProwritingAid can also be used to protect yourself from being accused of plagiarizing when using Google Docs to create documents. The Tool automatically identifies all document elements that are similar to others and gives you a mark indicating whether these elements are copied or similar.

Difference Between Grammarly & ProWritingAid
There is no doubt that both ProwritingAid and Grammarly can help with your English grammar. However, these programs differ significantly in how much they can improve your writing skills. Therefore, it is important to consider the differences between these programs when deciding which one you should use for your needs. You also need to know what kind of writer you are and how the two programs will best suit you.

Comparison between Grammarly and ProWritingAid: Grammarly is a widely-used writing enhancement tool. It offers numerous styles, suggestions for grammar and vocabulary. ProWritingAid is the best grammar- and style-checking Tool that also includes more detailed reports to help strengthen your writing.

Any discussion of ProWritingAid vs Grammarly would not be complete with this.

Based on our tests, Grammarly is 100% accurate for nonfiction writing. ProWritingAid is a great option for fiction writers and accurate reports. Even the free version of Grammarly offers many features. On the other hand, the free version of PWA has limited features. Grammarly has a better way of handling accuracy. However, ProWritingAid can be used to help you with fragmented sentences, dialogue, and more. It can also be helpful for fiction writers. Grammarly is simple to use and has a clean interface. Unfortunately, the ProWritingAid interface doesn’t look as nice as Grammarly. Grammarly allows you to identify any writing errors in content. In addition, ProWritingAid can handle fragmented sentences. Grammarly costs more than ProWritingAid.

This grammar-checker Tool can also be used on iOS and Android phones. Unfortunately, ProWritingAid (PWA) doesn’t support mobile apps for iOS or Android.

①Privileged Account > ②Standard Account(DML+DDL) > ③Standard Account(DDL) > ④Standard Account(DML) > ⑤Standard Account(Read-Only)

① How to create privileged account?

Create on the console.

② How to create standard account with DDL+DML privileges? See example as below: The given example will create a user named 'normal3' with DDL and DML privileges on DB 'test3' and it is allowed to access from anywhere. `create user IF NOT EXISTS normal3@'%' identified by 'Passw@rd!';

show grants for normal3;

GRANT PROCESS, REPLICATION SLAVE, REPLICATION CLIENT ON . TO 'normal3'@'%';
GRANT SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, CREATE, DROP, INDEX, ALTER, CREATE TEMPORARY TABLES, LOCK TABLES, EXECUTE, CREATE VIEW, SHOW VIEW, CREATE ROUTINE, ALTER ROUTINE, EVENT, TRIGGER ON test3.* TO 'normal3'@'%';

GRANT SELECT ON mysql.time_zone_name TO 'normal3'@'%';
GRANT SELECT ON mysql.help_category TO 'normal3'@'%';
GRANT SELECT ON mysql.help_relation TO 'normal3'@'%';
GRANT SELECT ON mysql.time_zone_leap_second TO 'normal3'@'%';
GRANT SELECT ON mysql.slow_log TO 'normal3'@'%';
GRANT SELECT ON mysql.time_zone_transition_type TO 'normal3'@'%';
GRANT SELECT ON mysql.func TO 'normal3'@'%';
GRANT SELECT ON mysql.general_log TO 'normal3'@'%';
GRANT SELECT ON mysql.proc TO 'normal3'@'%';
GRANT SELECT ON mysql.time_zone TO 'normal3'@'%';
GRANT SELECT ON mysql.help_topic TO 'normal3'@'%';
GRANT SELECT ON mysql.time_zone_transition TO 'normal3'@'%';
GRANT SELECT ON mysql.event TO 'normal3'@'%';`

③ How to create standard account with DDL privileges?

create user IF NOT EXISTS normal6@'%' identified by 'Passw@rd!'; GRANT PROCESS, REPLICATION SLAVE, REPLICATION CLIENT ON *.* TO 'normal6'@'%';

Items.Title: 宣布 Chakra UI Svelte | Announcing Chakra UI Svelte
Items.Description:
乔纳森·伊罗迪亚

发表于我很高兴能分享我在 4 月份的大部分时间里花费的时间构建的东西 - 在 svelte 中实现 Chakra UI。 这是流行的Chakra UI的实现，就像我们对Vue的实现一样。 我目前正在为它编写适当的文档。 但是，该包遵循 Chakra UI React 样式指南。

什么是脉轮用户界面？

去年，当我第一次尝试该框架时，我爱上了 Chakra UI。 作为一个苗条的爱好者，我一直在寻找苗条的实现，但没有找到。 所以，我开始着手创建它。

我花了整整一个月的时间来测试组件和检查样式实践。 我也在研究Chakra UI React source 。 我希望实现与 React 密切相关。 目前，我已经完成了大约 13 个组件。

发布信息

当前稳定的预发布版本：0.0.2-next.2
新PM

入门

要开始，只需安装包

您可以选择安装 svelte-icons 包。 这是迄今为止我用 chakra-ui-svelte 测试过的唯一图标包。 它相当稳定，我有计划在以后完全支持它。

示例应用程序

让我们尝试构建一个示例应用程序，好吗？ 首先，我们需要导入处理主题和其他全局配置的 ChakraProvider 组件。 

 import { ChakraProvider } from 'chakra-ui-svelte';

现在我们可以继续使用提供程序来处理 chakra-ui-svelte 默认提供的一些商店。 并导入其他包

<script> import { Box , Button , ChakraProvider } from ' chakra-ui-svelte ' ; </script> <ChakraProvider> <Box> Hello Box </Box> <Button> Hello Button </Button> </ChakraProvider>

样式组件

我们也可以像在 Chakra Ui React 中一样自定义我们的组件。

 <script> import { Button , Icon } from ' chakra-ui-svelte ' ; import FaGithub from " svelte-icons/fa/FaGithub.svelte " ; </script> <Button as= "a" p= "0" mx= "3" size= "xs" bg= "transparent" href= {"https://github.com/elcharitas/chakra-ui-svelte"} target= "_blank" rel= "noreferrer" ariaLabel= "Chakra UI on Github" variant= "ghost" > <Icon as= {FaGithub} /> </Button>

最后的想法？

可悲的是，在撰写本文时没有任何适当的文档。 但是，这里有一个演示。 我将在下周用文档更新页面。
请留下反馈并随时为软件包做出贡献。

首次发布在这里

克里斯蒂安·费尔南多—— Apr 27 4月27日



原文:

Jonathan Irhodia

Posted on May 7

I'm so excited to share what I spent the better part of April building - Chakra UI implementation in svelte. This is an implementation of the popular Chakra UI just as we have for Vue. I'm currently working on proper documentation for it. However, the package follows the Chakra UI React style guide.

What is Chakra UI?

Chakra UI is a simple, modular and accessible component library that gives you the building blocks you need to build your React applications. I fell in love with Chakra UI last year when I first tried the framework. Being more of a svelte lover, I was looking for implementation in svelte but didn't find any. So, I set out to create it.

It took me a whole month of testing components and checking style practices. I was also looking into Chakra UI React source. I wanted the implementation to be so closely related to React. And at the moment, I've completed about 13 components.

Release Info

Current Stable Prerelease Version: 0.0.2-next.2
Github
NPM

Getting Started

To get started, simply install the package with

You may optionally install the svelte-icons package. It's the only icon package I've tested so far with chakra-ui-svelte. It's fairly stable and I have the plan of fully supporting it later on.

Sample Application

Let's try building a sample app, shall we? First, we need to import the ChakraProvider component which handles themes and other global configurations.

import { ChakraProvider } from 'chakra-ui-svelte';

Now we can go on ahead and use the provider to handle some of the stores which chakra-ui-svelte provides by default. and import other packages

<script> import { Box, Button, ChakraProvider } from 'chakra-ui-svelte';
</script> <ChakraProvider> <Box>Hello Box</Box> <Button> Hello Button </Button>
</ChakraProvider>

Styling Components

We can also customize our components just like we do in Chakra Ui React.

<script> import { Button, Icon } from 'chakra-ui-svelte'; import FaGithub from "svelte-icons/fa/FaGithub.svelte";
</script>
<Button as="a" p="0" mx="3" size="xs" bg="transparent" href={"https://github.com/elcharitas/chakra-ui-svelte"} target="_blank" rel="noreferrer" ariaLabel="Chakra UI on Github" variant="ghost"
> <Icon as={FaGithub} />
</Button>

Final Thoughts?

Sadly, there isn't any proper documentation at the time of this writing. However, a demo is available here. I'd be updating the page with documentation in the coming week.
Please leave feedback and feel free to contribute to the package.

First published here

Cristian Fernando - Apr 27

Items.Title: 更好的 CSV 导入 | A better CSV import
Items.Description:
如果您曾经不得不从 Web 应用程序中的 CSV 文件导入数据，那么您的体验很可能不是很好。 您需要准备一个包含所需列的文件，导出到 .csv 并确保分隔符正确。 乐趣不止于此。 如果您的数据中有任何错误，例如格式错误或缺少值，应用程序会告诉您在继续之前修复它。 现在，您需要返回 Excel 或 Google 表格并找到错误的单元格。 冲洗并重复。

在为我们的开源和自托管商店查找器Mapzy构建批量位置导入功能时，我们希望为我们的用户提供更好的导入体验。 我们想让他们免于处理导出文件和选择分隔符的痛苦，并且我们想让他们更容易地修复任何导入错误。

在一项只能比作第一代 iPhone 的发明中，我们提出了以下建议：在导入屏幕中，我们提供了一个嵌入的电子表格，该电子表格已经填写了正确的标题。 用户无需从计算机中选择文件，只需将其 CSV 数据复制并粘贴到此电子表格中，然后按下红色大按钮即可。 如果有任何错误，我们只需突出显示电子表格中的相关单元格。 这样，用户可以轻松地立即修复它们，而无需摆弄单独的文件并再次完成整个过程。

女士们，先生们，不用多说，我向您介绍 - Mapzy 位置导入器：

如果有错误，我们会这样处理：

对于那些对技术背景感兴趣的人，更多细节如下。

对于电子表格，我们使用优秀的Jspreadsheet库。 如果没有错误，则导入分两步进行，因为导入大量位置需要对 Mapbox 地理编码 API 进行大量调用（地理编码是将地址转换为经度和纬度），这是速率限制的。

在第一步中，我们只验证信息是否全部存在并且格式正确。 例如，如果缺少位置名称，我们会在电子表格中突出显示该错误。 如果一切看起来都不错，我们使用很棒的Activerecord-Import库将位置保存到数据库中，但跳过调用地理编码 API。 相反，我们将这些位置标记为“要进行地理编码”。

在第二步中，我们然后启动一个后台作业（使用 Sidekiq 和 Redis），该作业遍历数据库并检查与给定地图相关的所有位置，并在必要时对其地址进行地理编码。 如果地理编码尝试不成功，我们稍后会要求用户从他们的 Mapzy 仪表板中更正地址。

如果您对代码感兴趣，请查看location_imports_controller.rb 、 location_import.rb和batch_geocode_worker.rb 。

这种方法的另一个技术优势是我们不需要处理从 CSV 文件到我们的数据模型的映射列，这本身可能是一整罐蠕虫。



原文:

If you ever had to import data from a CSV file in a web app, there's a high chance that your experience wasn't great. You need to prepare a file with the required columns, export to .csv and make sure to get the delimiter right. (Ironically, even though CSV stands for comma separated values, the delimiter can be a different character like a semicolon or tab). The fun doesn't stop there. If there are any errors in your data, such as wrong formatting or missing values, the app will tell you to fix it before continuing. Now, you need to go back to Excel or Google Sheets and find the erroneous cells. Rinse and repeat.

When building the bulk location import feature for Mapzy, our open-source and self-hostable store finder, we wanted to provide a better import experience for our users. We wanted to spare them the pain of dealing with exporting files and choosing delimiters, and we wanted to make it simple for them to fix any import errors.

In an innovation that can only be likened to the invention of the first iPhone, we came up with the following: In the import screen, we provide an embedded spreadsheet that already has the correct headers filled out. Instead of choosing a file from their computer, users just copy and paste their CSV data into this spreadsheet and press the big red button. If there are any errors, we simply highlight the relevant cells in the spreadsheet. This way, users can easily fix them right then and there without needing to fiddle with a separate file and go through the whole process again.

Ladies and gentlemen, without further ado, I present to you - the Mapzy location importer:

If there are errors, we handle them like this:

For those who are interested in the technical background, more details follow.

For the spreadsheet, we use the excellent Jspreadsheet library. If there are no errors, the import happens in two steps because importing a lot of locations requires a lot of calls to the Mapbox geocoding API (geocoding is converting an address to longitude and latitude), which is rate limited.

In the first step, we only validate if the information is all there and correctly formatted. For example, if a location name is missing, we highlight that error in the spreadsheet. If everything looks good, we save the locations to the database using the awesome Activerecord-Import library, but skip calling the geocoding API. Rather, we mark these locations as "to be geocoded".

In the second step, we then start a background job (with Sidekiq and Redis) that goes through the database and checks all locations that are related to the given map and geocodes their addresses if necessary. If a geocoding attempt is not successful, we later ask the user to correct the address from their Mapzy dashboard.

If you're interested in the code, have a look at location_imports_controller.rb, location_import.rb, and batch_geocode_worker.rb.

An added technical bonus of this approach is that we don't need to deal with mapping columns from a CSV file to our data models, which can be a whole can of worms in itself.
Items.Author: canolcer

Items.Title: 我们应该如何使用数据集成框架来打破数据服务全生命周期实现的困境？ | How should we use data integration frameworks to break the dilemma of full lifecycle implementation of data services?
Items.Description:
随着我们进入数字经济，公司和工程师都敏锐地意识到数据在生产中的重要性日益增加。 大数据被称为数字时代的“石油”，蕴含着巨大的价值和可能性，有待探索和利用。

众所周知，数据的生命周期分为采集、存储、集成、分析应用、归档、销毁等阶段。 数据集成是数据生命周期中的关键环节，决定了企业能否以最低的成本从数据中心获得最大的价值。

随着数据资源成为生产、发展乃至生存的基本要素，数据集成如何帮助企业实现数据服务的全生命周期？ 本次以“数据服务全生命周期实现实践”为主题的Meetup，来自游族、腾讯、Whaleops等企业的资深工程师将围绕网络数据接入平台的设计与实现、面向消息的中间件的改进、API重构等话题展开讨论以及基于Apache SeaTunnel（Incubating）和Apache InLong（Incubating）的PCG大数据支撑系统的实现和优化等课题，以他们在开发实践中遇到的困难和收获为蓝本，推荐相应的解决方案和摘要。

作为下一代高性能分布式海量数据集成框架，Apache SeaTunnel（Incubating）致力于降低学习成本，加速分布式数据处理能力在生产环境中的部署。 而Apache InLong（Incubating）是海量数据的一站式集成框架，提供自动化、安全、可靠的数据传输能力。

这两款优秀的开源产品在数据服务领域会发生什么？ 加入我们 5 月 14 日在线的 Apache SeaTunnel（孵化）和 Apache InLong（孵化）联合聚会，与演讲者互动。 您的问题将在这里得到解答！

形式：线上聚会

点击链接免费注册： https ://www.slidestalk.com/m/777

    原文:


    As we enter the digital economy, both companies and engineers are acutely aware of the increasing importance of data in production. Big data, known as the “oil” of the digital age, holds tremendous value and possibilities that are waiting to be explored and utilized.

    As we all know, the life cycle of data is divided into stages such as collection, storage, integration, analysis and application, archiving, and destruction. Data integration is a crucial process of the data lifecycle, which is the determinant to whether the enterprise can get the most value from the data center with the least cost.

    As data resources become an essential element of production, development, and even survival, how can data integration help enterprises implement the full lifecycle of data services? On May 14, we will be joined by technical experts and open source contributors from Apache SeaTunnel(Incubating) and Apache InLong(Incubating) to talk about their practical experiences and insights on this topic.

    In this Meetup themed on “Data Service Full Lifecycle Implementation Practice”, senior engineers from enterprises such as Yoozoo, Tencent, and Whaleops will talk about the design and implementations in the network data access platforms, the improvement of message-oriented middlewares, API refactoring and the implementation and optimization of the PCG big data support system, and other topics based on Apache SeaTunnel (Incubating) and Apache InLong (Incubating), with the difficulties and gains encountered in their development practice as a blueprint, and recommend the corresponding solutions and summaries.

    Apache SeaTunnel (Incubating) and Apache InLong (Incubating) are both excellent projects in the Apache open-source community. As the next-generation, high-performance, distributed, mass data integration framework, Apache SeaTunnel (Incubating) is dedicated to reducing learning costs and accelerating the deployment of distributed data processing capabilities in production environments. While Apache InLong (Incubating) is a one-stop integration framework for massive amounts of data, providing automated, secure, and reliable data transfer capabilities.

    What will happen between these two excellent open-source products in the data services space? Join us at the Apache SeaTunnel (Incubating) & Apache InLong (Incubating) Joint Meetup online on May 14 to interact with the speakers. Your questions will be answered here!

    01 Registration

    The registration for the Apache SeaTunnel(Incubating) & Apache InLong(Incubating) Joint Meetup | May 14 is open, register for free now!

    Time: 2022–5–14 14:00–17:00

    Format: Online meetup

    Click on the link to register for free: https://www.slidestalk.com/m/777

    Join the Slack group: https://join.slack.com/t/apacheseatunnel/shared_invite/zt-18lmwijxg-kYXQTOuDBBzfXR64nlPj9A

    02 Schedule

    Xiaodong Zhang, Big Data Development Engineer | YOOZOO

    Responsible for the data platform construction of the data center, mainly the development of the data access platform.

    Time: 14:05–14:50

    Speech Topic: Design and Implementation of Apache SeaTunnel(Incubating) in YOOZOO Data Access Platform

    Introduce how to integrate Apache SeaTunnel(Incubating) into the data access platform and the achievement.

    Guocheng Zhang, Senior Engineer | Tencent Data Platform Department

    Responsible for the development of the data access platform

    Time: 14:50–15:20

    Speech Topic: Improvement of Apache InLong (Incubating) on ​​message middleware

    Introduce the challenges encountered by the data access platform when the average daily message access exceeds one trillion pieces, the improvement plans, and effects.

    15:20–15:30 Lucky Draw Time

    Li Zongwen, Senior Engineer, Apache SeaTunnel(Incubating) & Flink Contributor, Flink CDC & Debezium Contributor | WhaleOps

    Time: 15:30–16:15

    Speech Topic: Apache SeaTunnel(Incubating) API refactoring

    Why do we need to refactor the Apache SeaTunnel(Incubating) API? How to refactor Apache SeaTunnel(Incubating) API?

    Lu Chunliang, Senior Engineer | Tencent PCG

    Responsible for the development of the basic big data support system for Tencent PCG big data collection, reporting, transmission, and distribution.

    Time: 16:15–17:00

    Speech Topic: Implementation and optimization of Apache InLong (Incubating) in Tencent PCG big data support system

    Introduce Apache InLong(Incubating)’s protection and automation measures to deal with abnormal failures and traffic shocks, reduce loss and repetition rate, and control O&M investment.

    The Apache SeaTunnel(Incubating) & Apache InLong(Incubating) Joint Meetup will be online on May 14 at 14:00, and we look forward to seeing you later!

    About SeaTunnel

    SeaTunnel (formerly Waterdrop) is an easy-to-use, ultra-high-performance distributed data integration platform that supports real-time synchronization of massive amounts of data and can synchronize hundreds of billions of data per day in a stable and efficient manner.

    Why do we need SeaTunnel?

    SeaTunnel does everything it can to solve the problems you may encounter in synchronizing massive amounts of data.

    • Data loss and duplication
    • Task buildup and latency
    • Low throughput
    • Long application-to-production cycle time
    • Lack of application status monitoring

    SeaTunnel Usage Scenarios

    • Massive data synchronization
    • Massive data integration
    • ETL of large volumes of data
    • Massive data aggregation
    • Multi-source data processing

    Features of SeaTunnel

    • Rich components
    • High scalability
    • Easy to use
    • Mature and stable

    How to get started with SeaTunnel quickly?

    Want to experience SeaTunnel quickly? SeaTunnel 2.1.0 takes 10 seconds to get you up and running.

    https://seatunnel.apache.org/docs/2.1.0/developement/setup

    How can I contribute?

    We invite all partners who are interested in making local open-source global to join the SeaTunnel contributors family and foster open-source together!

Items.Title: $PSScriptRoot 是你的朋友... | $PSScriptRoot is your friend...
Items.Description:
太极度

发表于昨天不得不艰难地学习这个教训。 我正在编写一个脚本，该脚本使用脚本所在文件夹的相对路径。

我想使用 jenkins Job 中的脚本。 该作业从与脚本自己的文件夹不同的文件夹执行脚本，并且突然找不到任何相对路径。

签出此脚本 doSource.ps1：

 #The Path asscociated with the . changes when you run it from another folder
Resolve-Path . #Script Root doesn't change
Resolve-Path $PSScriptRoot

当我运行它时会发生什么：

 PS F:\data\ps-scripts > . \doSource.ps1 Path
----
F:\data\ps-scripts F:\data\ps-scripts PS F:\data\ps-scripts > cd..
PS F:\data > . \ps-scripts\doSource.ps1 Path
----
F:\data F:\data\ps-scripts PS F:\data >



原文:

taijidude

Posted on May 7

Had to learn this Lessons the hard way yesterday. I was working on a script that uses relative paths to the folder where the script is.

I want to use the script from a jenkins Job. The job executed the script from different folder than the scripts own folder and suddenly none of the relative paths where found.

Checkout this script doSource.ps1:

#The Path asscociated with the . changes when you run it from another folder
Resolve-Path . #Script Root doesn't change
Resolve-Path $PSScriptRoot

And what happens when i run it:

PS F:\data\ps-scripts> .\doSource.ps1 Path
----
F:\data\ps-scripts F:\data\ps-scripts PS F:\data\ps-scripts> cd..
PS F:\data> .\ps-scripts\doSource.ps1 Path
----
F:\data F:\data\ps-scripts PS F:\data>
Items.Author: taijidude

Items.Title: 如何创建和测试生成 Supabase 数据库类型的 GitHub Action | How to create and test a GitHub Action that generates Supabase database types
Items.Description:
在 Supbase 文档中，有一个关于生成类型的页面。 按照说明，要运行的脚本看起来像这样。

请注意附加的--version=2参数。 在对openapi-typescript工具进行了一些更新之后，这可能成为一个强制性的道具。

 npx openapi-typescript https:// { projectId } .supabase.co/rest/v1/?apikey ={ specialApiKey } --output = types/supabase.ts --version = 2

匿名键和 URL 的值可以在 Supabase 仪表板中找到。

运行脚本会生成一个像这样的文件。 使用那里生成的定义，我可以只使用下面的代码块为Bill创建一个类型。

 import { definitions } from ' ../types/supabase ' ; export type Bill = definitions [ ' Bill ' ];

更改类型后，我的大部分项目源代码仍然可以正常工作。 我必须重构的唯一代码与 Date 类型的deadline字段有关。 定义将字段声明为字符串，即使他们确实承认它是日期格式。

 Bill : { /** Format: date */ deadline ?: string ; }

我个人认为这没问题，因为这会迫使您将日期对象转换为适当的 ISO 字符串，因此将您在 JS 中构建的 Date 对象传递给 Supabase 的歧义较少。 如果你不喜欢这个，你可以在你自己声明的类型中覆盖它。

在同一个文档中，他们还包括一个关于如何使用 GitHub Actions 自动更新类型的部分。 这可确保每当您更新数据库时，您的 GitHub 操作都会自动更新您在数据库中拥有的类型。 然后，下次您提取源代码时，您会知道有哪些新更改以及如何适应它们。

在文档中，有一个脚本要添加到package.json中。

 "update-types" : "npx openapi-typescript https://your-project.supabase.co/rest/v1/?apikey=your-anon-key --output types/database/index.ts"

但是，当我推送源代码时，我不太确定如何用环境变量安全地替换 package.json 脚本中的值。 我对 bash 语法不是很熟悉，但我认为脚本对于我的项目应该看起来像这样。

 "update-types" : : "npx openapi-typescript https://${SUPABASE_URL}/rest/v1/?apikey=${ANON_KEY} --version=2 --output types/database/index.ts"

为了再次确认语法，我决定使用 Sourcegraph Visual Studio Code 插件来查找已实现此 GitHub 操作的项目。

与 Sourcegraph Visual Studio Code 插件的交叉引用代码

安装后，您可以在侧边栏找到 Sourcegraph 图标。 当您单击它时，您将看到执行通用代码搜索的常用 UI。 要查找也生成类型的其他项目，我们可以传入查询npx openapi-typescript 。

我们看到了很多结果，幸运的是，在名为tone-row/flowchart-fun的第三个项目中，我看到他们的package.json脚本看起来很有希望。 它们有SB_URL和SB_ANON_KEY类的变量，类似于 Supbase 的东西✨

 "generate:types" : "export $(cat .env.local | xargs) && npx openapi-typescript \" ${SB_URL}/rest/v1/?apikey=${SB_ANON_KEY} \" --output types/database/index.ts" ,

有了这种交叉引用和知识，我们知道我们错过了将整个 API 端点包装在带有转义字符的字符串中。 然后，我们可以修改我们之前的脚本来匹配这个脚本。

 "update-types" : "npx openapi-typescript \" ${SUPABASE_URL}/rest/v1/?apikey=${SUPABASE_ANON_KEY} \" --version=2 --output types/database/index.ts"

如果我们通过npm run update-types在本地运行此脚本，我们可以看到它生成的输出与我们之前所做的相同✨

出于好奇，我还想知道他们的 GitHub 工作流程是否遵循 Supbase 文档中给出的示例中给出的相同结构。 很酷的是使用 Sourcegraph 插件，我可以在编辑器本身中探索存储库。

遗憾的是，这个项目没有使用他们在 GitHub 工作流程中编写的generate:types脚本。 他们可能只在本地运行 npm 脚本。 值得庆幸的是，在 Supabase 文档人员的帮助下，至少我们不必从头开始编写动作。

如何创建 GitHub 工作流

在本文发表后，此部分已在此处合并到 Supabase 文档中。

要将 GitHub 操作工作流添加到您的项目，您需要一个新文件夹.github/workflows来存储工作流。 对于包含 GitHub 操作工作流步骤的workflow.yml ，我们将使用 Supbase 文档中的示例 GitHub 操作工作流作为基础。 我稍微修改了一下，插入了一些 GitHub Secrets 作为环境变量，你可以参考下面的评论。

 name : Update database types on : schedule : # sets the action to run daily. You can modify this to run the action more or less frequently - cron : ' 0 0 * * *' # for the workflow to be dispatched manually via GitHub actions dashboards. workflow_dispatch : jobs : update : runs-on : ubuntu-latest env : SUPABASE_URL : ${{secrets.SUPABASE_URL}} SUPABASE_ANON_KEY : ${{secrets.SUPABASE_URL}} steps : - uses : actions/checkout@v2 with : persist-credentials : false fetch-depth : 0 - uses : actions/setup-node@v2.1.5 with : node-version : 14 # -- add echo -- # - run : | npm run update-types echo "$(cat types/database/index.ts)" - name : check for file changes id : git_status run : | echo "::set-output name=status::$(git status -s)" - name : Commit files if : ${{contains(steps.git_status.outputs.status, ' ')}} # -- change git stage filename -- # run : | git add types/database/index.ts git config --local user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com" git config --local user.name "billy-github-actions[bot]" git commit -m "Update database types" -a - name : Push changes if : ${{contains(steps.git_status.outputs.status, ' ')}} uses : ad-m/github-push-action@master with : github_token : ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }} branch : ${{ github.ref }}

这些变化包括：

  • 添加触发器workflow_dispatch以便在必要时可以通过 GitHub 操作仪表板手动分派工作流。
  • 添加echo语句以查看运行update-types脚本的结果文件的内容
  • 将 GitHub 操作机密传递到环境变量中。
  • 更改要暂存的文件名

如果您不熟悉 GitHub 操作机密，请将其视为仅用于 GitHub 操作的单独环境变量。 这是您可以在 GitHub 项目的设置中添加它们的屏幕截图。

要部署工作流，只需推送.github/workflows文件夹。

然后，您将在 GitHub 项目的 Actions 选项卡中看到可用的新工作流程。 当您单击附加选项按钮时，您可以手动分派工作流。

如果遇到任何问题，您还可以查看每个工作流运行的日志。 例如，在成功的作业中，我可以看到检索到的src/types/supabase.ts文件内容，因为我们将其作为工作流步骤的一部分注销。

在下一节中，我将向您介绍我在构建自定义复合 GitHub 操作以在本地测试 GitHub 操作时学到的一个方便的工具。

如何使用 act 在本地测试 GitHub Actions

这样做的好处是您可以获得更快的反馈，而不会阻塞项目的 GitHub Action Runner 历史记录。

请参阅GitHub 存储库 README.md以了解如何安装act 。 你也需要安装 Docker。 第一次启动 act 时，系统会要求您选择要创建的图像。 对于这个 GitHub Action，要测试更新数据库类型的功能，微图像应该足够了。

完成此配置后，您可以导航到~/.actrc以查看 docker 映像的配置。

 -P ubuntu-latest = node:16-buster-slim -P ubuntu-20.04 = node:16-buster-slim -P ubuntu-18.04 = node:16-buster-slim

请注意，Micro 映像不具备 GitHub Action 运行器的全部功能，因此您会看到与 git 命令相关的错误。 我不想用大图像(~20GB)来炸毁我的计算机内存，但如果你愿意，你可以试试。

要使用act在本地运行此工作流程，我们可以运行此 bash 命令。

 act -j update --secret-file .env


我们可以传入以下参数：

参数说明

  • -j ${jobName}
    • 我们在这个工作流中只声明了 1 个作业，所以我们可以通过更新作业

  • --secret-file ${filePath}

    • 对于该项目，我已经将.env用于所有环境变量，因此我可以将同一个文件传递给秘密。
     SUPABASE_URL = your_url SUPABASE_ANON_KEY = hehe
    • 如果您出于某种原因不想在本地拥有 .env 文件，您也可以使用参数-s SUPBASE_URL=your_url -s SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=hehe手动传递变量

运行 bash 命令后，这里是终端输出的简化摘录

[ Update database types/update] ✅ Success - actions/setup-node@v2.1.5 [ Update database types/update] ⭐ Run npm run update-types echo " $( cat src/types/supabase.ts ) " [ Update database types/update] 🐳 docker exec cmd =[ bash --noprofile --norc -e -o pipefail /var/run/act/workflow/2] user = workdir = | | > billy@0.0.1 update-types /Users/lyqht/self-study/Billy | > npx openapi-typescript " ${ SUPABASE_URL } /rest/v1/?apikey= ${ SUPABASE_ANON_KEY } " --version = 2 --output src/types/supabase.ts | | npx: installed 10 in 3.328s | ✨ openapi-typescript 5.2.0 🚀 *** /rest/v1/?apikey = *** -> /Users/lyqht/self-study/Billy/src/types/supabase.ts [ 28ms] | / ** | * This file was auto-generated by openapi-typescript. | * Do not make direct changes to the file. | * / | | export interface paths { | "/" : { | get: { | responses: { | / ** OK * / | 200: unknown ; | } ; | } ; | } ; | "/Bill" : { | get: { | .... } // i won 't show you all of it, but you will be able to see all these [Update database types/update] ✅ Success - npm run update-types echo "$(cat src/types/supabase.ts)" [Update database types/update] ⭐ Run check for file changes [Update database types/update] 🐳 docker exec cmd=[bash --noprofile --norc -e -o pipefail /var/run/act/workflow/git_status] user= workdir= | /var/run/act/workflow/git_status: line 2: git: command not found [Update database types/update] ⚙ ::set-output:: status= [Update database types/update] ✅ Success - check for file changes

如果您运行它，您可以看到输出包含“/Bill”的路径部分，这是运行update-types脚本的结果。 这意味着它工作正常🎉

奖金：在行动

自从发布原始文章后，我还创建了一个 GitHub 操作来执行上面列出的操作。 看看这里！

伙计们，这是一个包装！ 感谢您阅读，希望您喜欢这篇文章！

如果你觉得这篇文章很棒，请点击反应🧡并分享它🐦~

要在我发布新内容时保持更新，请在Twitter 上关注我。



原文:

On the Supabase documentation, there is a page on generating types. Following the instructions, the script to run looks something like this.

Note the additional --version=2 parameter. This probably became a mandatory prop after some update to the openapi-typescript tool.

npx openapi-typescript https://{projectId}.supabase.co/rest/v1/?apikey={specialApiKey} --output=types/supabase.ts --version=2

The values for the anon key and URL can be found in the Supabase dashboard.

Running the script generates a file like this. With the definitions that are generated there, I could create a type for Bill with just the code block below.

import {definitions} from '../types/supabase'; export type Bill = definitions['Bill'];

Most of my project source code still works fine after changing the type. The only code I had to refactor is related to the deadline field which is Date type. The definitions has the field declared as a string, even though they do acknowledge it is a Date format.

Bill: { /** Format: date */ deadline?: string;
}

Personally I think this is ok, since this forces you to convert date objects to appropriate ISO strings, so there is less ambiguity about passing the Date object that you have constructed in JS to Supabase. If you don’t like this you could override it in your self-declared type.

In the same documentation, they also included a section on how to update types automatically with GitHub Actions. This ensures that whenever you update the database, your GitHub action will automatically update the types that you have in your DB. Then, the next time you pull your source code, you would know what are the new changes and how to accommodate them.

In the docs, there’s a script to be added to the package.json as such.

"update-types": "npx openapi-typescript https://your-project.supabase.co/rest/v1/?apikey=your-anon-key --output types/database/index.ts"

However, I wasn’t too sure how to replace the values in the package.json script safely with environmental variables when I push the source code. I’m not very familiar with bash syntax, but I assume the script should look something like this for my project.

"update-types": : "npx openapi-typescript https://${SUPABASE_URL}/rest/v1/?apikey=${ANON_KEY} --version=2 --output types/database/index.ts"

To double confirm the syntax, I decided to use the Sourcegraph Visual Studio Code plugin to look for projects that have implemented this GitHub action.

Cross-reference code with Sourcegraph Visual Studio Code plugin

The Sourcegraph Visual Studio Code plugin can be installed through the Extensions panel.

Once you have it installed, you can find the Sourcegraph icon at the side bar. When you click it, you will see the usual UI to do a universal code search. To find other projects that also generate types, we can pass in a query npx openapi-typescript.

We see quite a few results, and luckily, in the 3rd project called tone-row/flowchart-fun, I see that they have package.json script that looks like a promising candidate. They have variables such as SB_URL and SB_ANON_KEY which resemble Supabase stuff ✨

"generate:types": "export $(cat .env.local | xargs) && npx openapi-typescript \"${SB_URL}/rest/v1/?apikey=${SB_ANON_KEY}\" --output types/database/index.ts",

With this cross-reference and knowledge, we know that we are missing out on wrapping the whole API endpoint in a string with escape characters. Then, we can modify our previous script to match this script.

"update-types": "npx openapi-typescript \"${SUPABASE_URL}/rest/v1/?apikey=${SUPABASE_ANON_KEY}\" --version=2 --output types/database/index.ts"

If we run this script locally via npm run update-types , we can see it generates the same output that we have done previously ✨

Out of curiosity, I also want to know if their GitHub workflow follows the same structure that was given in the example given in Supabase docs. The cool thing is with the Sourcegraph plugin, I can explore the repository in the editor itself.

Sadly, this project is not using the generate:types script that they have written in a GitHub workflow. They’re probably only running the npm script locally. Thankfully with the help of the Supabase documentation folks, at least we don’t have to write the action from scratch.

How to create the GitHub workflow

This section has been merged to Supabase documentation here after publication of this article.

To add GitHub action workflows to your project, you need a new folder .github/workflows to store the workflows. For the workflow.yml that consist the steps for the GitHub action workflow, we will be using the sample GitHub action workflow on the Supabase documentation as a base. I have modified it a little to insert some GitHub secrets as environment variables, you can refer to the comments below.

name: Update database types on: schedule: # sets the action to run daily. You can modify this to run the action more or less frequently - cron: '0 0 * * *' # for the workflow to be dispatched manually via GitHub actions dashboards. workflow_dispatch: jobs: update: runs-on: ubuntu-latest env: SUPABASE_URL: ${{secrets.SUPABASE_URL}} SUPABASE_ANON_KEY: ${{secrets.SUPABASE_URL}} steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v2 with: persist-credentials: false fetch-depth: 0 - uses: actions/setup-node@v2.1.5 with: node-version: 14 # -- add echo -- # - run: | npm run update-types  echo "$(cat types/database/index.ts)" - name: check for file changes id: git_status run: | echo "::set-output name=status::$(git status -s)" - name: Commit files if: ${{contains(steps.git_status.outputs.status, ' ')}} # -- change git stage filename -- # run: | git add types/database/index.ts git config --local user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com" git config --local user.name "billy-github-actions[bot]" git commit -m "Update database types" -a - name: Push changes if: ${{contains(steps.git_status.outputs.status, ' ')}} uses: ad-m/github-push-action@master with: github_token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }} branch: ${{ github.ref }}

The changes include:

  • Addition of a trigger workflow_dispatch so that the workflow can be dispatched manually via GitHub actions dashboards if necessary.
  • Addition of an echo statement to see the content of the result file from running update-types script
  • Pass GitHub action secrets into environment variables.
  • Change the filename to be staged

If you are not familiar with GitHub action secrets, think of it as separate environment variables solely for GitHub actions. This is a screenshot of where you can add them at the settings of your GitHub project.

To deploy the workflow, simply push the .github/workflows folder.

Then you will see the new workflow available in the Actions tab of your GitHub project. When you click on the additional options button, you can manually dispatch the workflow.

You can also view the logs of each workflow run if you encounter any issues. For example, here in the succeeded job, I can see the src/types/supabase.ts file content that is retrieved since we logged it out as part of the steps of the workflow.

In the next section, I’ll introduce you to a handy tool that I learnt while building a custom composite GitHub action to test GitHub actions locally.

How to test GitHub Actions locally with act

act is a tool that lets you test GitHub actions locally. The benefit of this is that you get faster feedback without clogging up the GitHub Action Runner history on your project.

Refer to the GitHub repository README.md to see how to install act. You would need Docker installed too. The first time you start up act, you will be asked to choose the image that you want to create. For this GitHub Action, to test the functionality of updating the database types, the Micro image should be sufficient.

After this config is completed, you can navigate to ~/.actrc to see the config for your docker image.

-P ubuntu-latest=node:16-buster-slim
-P ubuntu-20.04=node:16-buster-slim
-P ubuntu-18.04=node:16-buster-slim

Note that the Micro image does not have the full capability of a GitHub Action runner, so you will see errors related to git commands. I don’t want to explode my computer’s memory with the Large image (~20GB), but you can try if you like.

To run this workflow locally with act, we can run this bash command.

act -j update --secret-file .env


we can pass in the following parameters:

Explanation of parameters

  • -j ${jobName}
    • we only have 1 job declared in this workflow, so we can pass the update job

  • --secret-file ${filePath}

    • for the project, I am already using .env for all the environment variables, so I can pass this same file for secrets. 
    SUPABASE_URL=your_url
SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=hehe
    • If you rather not have .env files locally for some reason, you can also pass variables manually with the parameter -s SUPBASE_URL=your_url -s SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=hehe

After running the bash command, here’s a simplified extract of the terminal output

[Update database types/update] ✅ Success - actions/setup-node@v2.1.5
[Update database types/update] ⭐ Run npm run update-types
echo "$(cat src/types/supabase.ts)"
[Update database types/update] 🐳 docker exec cmd=[bash --noprofile --norc -e -o pipefail /var/run/act/workflow/2] user= workdir=
| | > billy@0.0.1 update-types /Users/lyqht/self-study/Billy
| > npx openapi-typescript "${SUPABASE_URL}/rest/v1/?apikey=${SUPABASE_ANON_KEY}" --version=2 --output src/types/supabase.ts
| | npx: installed 10 in 3.328s
| ✨ openapi-typescript 5.2.0
🚀 ***/rest/v1/?apikey=*** -> /Users/lyqht/self-study/Billy/src/types/supabase.ts [28ms]
| /**
| * This file was auto-generated by openapi-typescript.
| * Do not make direct changes to the file.
| */
| | export interface paths {
| "/": {
| get: {
| responses: {
| /** OK */
| 200: unknown;
| };
| };
| };
| "/Bill": {
| get: {
| .... } // i won't show you all of it, but you will be able to see all these
[Update database types/update] ✅ Success - npm run update-types
echo "$(cat src/types/supabase.ts)"
[Update database types/update] ⭐ Run check for file changes
[Update database types/update] 🐳 docker exec cmd=[bash --noprofile --norc -e -o pipefail /var/run/act/workflow/git_status] user= workdir=
| /var/run/act/workflow/git_status: line 2: git: command not found
[Update database types/update] ⚙ ::set-output:: status=
[Update database types/update] ✅ Success - check for file changes

If you run this, you can can see the output contains the paths portion for '/Bill' as a result of running the update-types script. This meant that it worked correctly 🎉

⚠️ Remember that these changes happen on the docker instance itself, you will not see any changes on your local folder.

Bonus: In Action

On 7 Apr 2022, I added 2 new tables, and my bot was a good boi.

Since publishing the original article, I also created a GitHub action that does what is listed above. Check it out here!

That's a wrap folks! 🎉

Thank you for reading, hope you enjoyed the article!

If you find the article awesome, hit the reactions 🧡 and share it 🐦~

To stay updated whenever I post new stuff, follow me on Twitter.

Items.Title: 我们应该如何使用数据集成框架来打破数据服务全生命周期实现的困境？ | How should we use data integration frameworks to break the dilemma of full lifecycle implementation of data services?
Items.Description:
随着我们进入数字经济，公司和工程师都敏锐地意识到数据在生产中的重要性日益增加。 大数据被称为数字时代的“石油”，蕴含着巨大的价值和可能性，有待探索和利用。

众所周知，数据的生命周期分为采集、存储、集成、分析应用、归档、销毁等阶段。 数据集成是数据生命周期中的关键环节，决定了企业能否以最低的成本从数据中心获得最大的价值。

随着数据资源成为生产、发展乃至生存的基本要素，数据集成如何帮助企业实现数据服务的全生命周期？ 本次以“数据服务全生命周期实现实践”为主题的Meetup，来自游族、腾讯、Whaleops等企业的资深工程师将围绕网络数据接入平台的设计与实现、面向消息的中间件的改进、API重构等话题展开讨论以及基于Apache SeaTunnel（Incubating）和Apache InLong（Incubating）的PCG大数据支撑系统的实现和优化等课题，以他们在开发实践中遇到的困难和收获为蓝本，推荐相应的解决方案和摘要。

作为下一代高性能分布式海量数据集成框架，Apache SeaTunnel（Incubating）致力于降低学习成本，加速分布式数据处理能力在生产环境中的部署。 而Apache InLong（Incubating）是海量数据的一站式集成框架，提供自动化、安全、可靠的数据传输能力。

这两款优秀的开源产品在数据服务领域会发生什么？ 参加 5 月 14 日在线 Apache SeaTunnel（孵化）和 Apache InLong（孵化）联合聚会，与演讲者互动。 您的问题将在这里得到解答！

时间：2022-5-14 14:00-17:00

形式：线上聚会

点击链接免费注册： https ://www.slidestalk.com/m/777

加入 Slack 群： https ://join.slack.com/t/apacheseatunnel/shared_invite/zt-18lmwijxg-kYXQTOuDBBzfXR64nlPj9A

张晓东，大数据开发工程师 | 游族

负责数据中心的数据平台建设，主要是数据接入平台的开发。

时间：14:05-14:50

演讲题目：游族数据接入平台Apache SeaTunnel（孵化）的设计与实现

介绍如何将Apache SeaTunnel(Incubating)集成到数据访问平台及成果。

张国成，高级工程师 | 腾讯数据平台部

负责数据接入平台的开发

时间：14:50–15:20

演讲题目：Apache InLong（Incubating）对消息中间件的改进

介绍数据访问平台在日均消息访问量超过万亿条时遇到的挑战、改进方案和效果。

李宗文，高级工程师，Apache SeaTunnel(Incubating) & Flink 贡献者，Flink CDC & Debezium 贡献者 | 鲸鱼行动

时间：15:30–16:15

演讲题目：Apache SeaTunnel(Incubating) API 重构

为什么我们需要重构 Apache SeaTunnel(Incubating) API？ 如何重构 Apache SeaTunnel(Incubating) API？

卢春亮，高级工程师 | 腾讯PCG

负责腾讯PCG大数据采集、上报、传输、分发的基础大数据支撑系统开发。

时间：16:15-17:00

演讲题目：Apache InLong（Incubating）在腾讯PCG大数据支撑系统中的实现与优化

引入Apache InLong(Incubating)的保护和自动化措施，应对异常故障和流量冲击，降低丢失和重复率，控制运维投入。

关于海隧道

为什么我们需要 SeaTunnel？

数据丢失和重复

  • 任务积累和延迟
  • 低吞吐量
  • 应用到生产周期长
  • 缺乏应用状态监控

    • 海量数据同步

  • 海量数据整合
  • 海量数据的ETL
  • 海量数据聚合
  • 多源数据处理

    • 海隧道的特点

    • 丰富的组件
    • 高扩展性
    • 便于使用
    • 成熟稳重

    如何快速开始使用 SeaTunnel？

    原文:


    As we enter the digital economy, both companies and engineers are acutely aware of the increasing importance of data in production. Big data, known as the “oil” of the digital age, holds tremendous value and possibilities that are waiting to be explored and utilized.

    As we all know, the life cycle of data is divided into stages such as collection, storage, integration, analysis and application, archiving, and destruction. Data integration is a crucial process of the data lifecycle, which is the determinant to whether the enterprise can get the most value from the data center with the least cost.

    As data resources become an essential element of production, development, and even survival, how can data integration help enterprises implement the full lifecycle of data services? On May 14, we will be joined by technical experts and open source contributors from Apache SeaTunnel(Incubating) and Apache InLong(Incubating) to talk about their practical experiences and insights on this topic.

    In this Meetup themed on “Data Service Full Lifecycle Implementation Practice”, senior engineers from enterprises such as Yoozoo, Tencent, and Whaleops will talk about the design and implementations in the network data access platforms, the improvement of message-oriented middlewares, API refactoring and the implementation and optimization of the PCG big data support system, and other topics based on Apache SeaTunnel (Incubating) and Apache InLong (Incubating), with the difficulties and gains encountered in their development practice as a blueprint, and recommend the corresponding solutions and summaries.

    Apache SeaTunnel (Incubating) and Apache InLong (Incubating) are both excellent projects in the Apache open-source community. As the next-generation, high-performance, distributed, mass data integration framework, Apache SeaTunnel (Incubating) is dedicated to reducing learning costs and accelerating the deployment of distributed data processing capabilities in production environments. While Apache InLong (Incubating) is a one-stop integration framework for massive amounts of data, providing automated, secure, and reliable data transfer capabilities.

    What will happen between these two excellent open-source products in the data services space? Join us at the Apache SeaTunnel (Incubating) & Apache InLong (Incubating) Joint Meetup online on May 14 to interact with the speakers. Your questions will be answered here!

    01 Registration

    The registration for the Apache SeaTunnel(Incubating) & Apache InLong(Incubating) Joint Meetup | May 14 is open, register for free now!

    Time: 2022–5–14 14:00–17:00

    Format: Online meetup

    Click on the link to register for free: https://www.slidestalk.com/m/777

    Join the Slack group: https://join.slack.com/t/apacheseatunnel/shared_invite/zt-18lmwijxg-kYXQTOuDBBzfXR64nlPj9A

    02 Schedule

    Xiaodong Zhang, Big Data Development Engineer | YOOZOO

    Responsible for the data platform construction of the data center, mainly the development of the data access platform.

    Time: 14:05–14:50

    Speech Topic: Design and Implementation of Apache SeaTunnel(Incubating) in YOOZOO Data Access Platform

    Introduce how to integrate Apache SeaTunnel(Incubating) into the data access platform and the achievement.

    Guocheng Zhang, Senior Engineer | Tencent Data Platform Department

    Responsible for the development of the data access platform

    Time: 14:50–15:20

    Speech Topic: Improvement of Apache InLong (Incubating) on ​​message middleware

    Introduce the challenges encountered by the data access platform when the average daily message access exceeds one trillion pieces, the improvement plans, and effects.

    15:20–15:30 Lucky Draw Time

    Li Zongwen, Senior Engineer, Apache SeaTunnel(Incubating) & Flink Contributor, Flink CDC & Debezium Contributor | WhaleOps

    Time: 15:30–16:15

    Speech Topic: Apache SeaTunnel(Incubating) API refactoring

    Why do we need to refactor the Apache SeaTunnel(Incubating) API? How to refactor Apache SeaTunnel(Incubating) API?

    Lu Chunliang, Senior Engineer | Tencent PCG

    Responsible for the development of the basic big data support system for Tencent PCG big data collection, reporting, transmission, and distribution.

    Time: 16:15–17:00

    Speech Topic: Implementation and optimization of Apache InLong (Incubating) in Tencent PCG big data support system

    Introduce Apache InLong(Incubating)’s protection and automation measures to deal with abnormal failures and traffic shocks, reduce loss and repetition rate, and control O&M investment.

    The Apache SeaTunnel(Incubating) & Apache InLong(Incubating) Joint Meetup will be online on May 14 at 14:00, and we look forward to seeing you later!

    About SeaTunnel

    SeaTunnel (formerly Waterdrop) is an easy-to-use, ultra-high-performance distributed data integration platform that supports real-time synchronization of massive amounts of data and can synchronize hundreds of billions of data per day in a stable and efficient manner.

    Why do we need SeaTunnel?

    SeaTunnel does everything it can to solve the problems you may encounter in synchronizing massive amounts of data.

    • Data loss and duplication
    • Task buildup and latency
    • Low throughput
    • Long application-to-production cycle time
    • Lack of application status monitoring

    SeaTunnel Usage Scenarios

    • Massive data synchronization
    • Massive data integration
    • ETL of large volumes of data
    • Massive data aggregation
    • Multi-source data processing

    Features of SeaTunnel

    • Rich components
    • High scalability
    • Easy to use
    • Mature and stable

    How to get started with SeaTunnel quickly?

Items.Title: Rubocop LTS | Rubocop LTS
Items.Description:
封面照片
埃伯哈德 🖐 格罗斯加斯泰格

我的上一篇文章讨论了流行的 ruby​​gem rubocop ，它缺乏SemVer （语义版本控制）遵守，并介绍了一组新的 gem，旨在减少由于不明智的rubocop升级而导致的构建失败。

但是切换到这种新范式需要每个项目管理与他们打算支持的最旧的 Ruby 匹配的rubocop-ruby*_*版本，这也意味着使用 Ruby 版本特定的字符串污染.rubocop.yml ，例如inherit_gem: rubocop-ruby2_4.yml 。 我们需要再次升级解决方案。

如果我们可以利用rubygems和bundler功能来解决依赖关系，并将其保存在一个单一的 gem 中，它只会在几乎没有风险的情况下更新。

我们可以将这个宝石rubocop-lts 。

它将遵循 SemVer，并且每个主要版本步骤都意味着一个 Ruby 版本被删除。 它将有一个静态 rubocop 配置，该配置将从rubocop-ruby*_*加载与 ruby​​ 匹配的底层配置，从而在升级 Ruby 时在 gemfile 中的主要版本之外导致零代码更改。 它将处理知道哪个版本的 Rubocop 是正确的。

如果这颗新宝石出现在您的眼前会怎样？

我正在这里处理它！ 请加入我！



原文:

Cover Photo by
eberhard 🖐 grossgasteiger

My last post addressed the popular rubygem rubocop, its lack of SemVer (Semantic Versioning) adherence, and introduced a new set of gems aimed at mitigating build failures due to ill-advised rubocop upgrades.

But switching to this new paradigm would require each project to manage the version of rubocop-ruby*_* that matches the oldest Ruby they intend to support, and this would also mean polluting .rubocop.yml with a Ruby version specific string, like inherit_gem: rubocop-ruby2_4.yml. We need to level up the solution one more time.

What if we could leverage rubygems and bundler abilities to resolve dependencies, and just keep it at one sigle gem, which would only update when there is little to no risk.

We could name this gem rubocop-lts.

It would follow SemVer, and each major version step would signify one version of Ruby being dropped. It would have a static rubocop config which would load the underlying config from rubocop-ruby*_* that matches the ruby, resulting in zero code changes outside bumping the major version in the gemfile when you go to upgrade Ruby. It will handle knowing which version of Rubocop is right.

What if this new gem materialized before your very eyes?

I'm working on it over here! Please join me!

Items.Title: 使用 RxJS 批处理事件 | Batching events with RxJS
Items.Description:
弗拉基米尔·伊万年科

发表于粗略地说，事件批处理是在一段时间内累积事件，以便稍后一次性处理它们。

我们可以采用两种众所周知的策略，甚至可以将它们结合起来以了解何时刷新和处理缓冲的一堆事件：

  • 当事件数量达到阈值时；
  • 使用间隔——例如每 10 秒刷新一次事件。

为什么使用它

事件批处理可能有利于：

  • 性能原因，例如，减少对持久存储的写入次数或优化通过网络传输的数据量；
  • 聚合——例如按链接分组并计算页面访问量。

如何

使用RxJS在 JavaScript 中实现事件批处理是不费吹灰之力的。

让我们从一个 Node 示例开始。

 const EventEmitter = require ( ' events ' ); const { fromEvent , bufferCount } = require ( ' rxjs ' ); // I assume you already have an instance of EventEmitter in your app. // In case I'm wrong, let's create the one. const eventEmitter = new EventEmitter (); // listen to an event called `something-good-happened` fromEvent ( eventEmitter , ' something-good-happened ' ) // accumulate events . pipe ( // and flush them every time it's number reaches 3 bufferCount ( 3 ), // let's log it tap (() => { console . log ( `Great! The number of good things happened in a row reached ${ events . length } . It's time to celebrate.` ); console . log ( events ); }) ) // process the batch . subscribe (( events ) => { const goodThingsByUser = {}; for ( const event of events ) { goodThingsByUser [ event . userId ] = ( goodThingsByUser [ event . userId ] ?? 0 ) + 1 ; } // reportGoodThingsDone(goodThingsByUser); });

当然，还有一个浏览器的例子。

 import { fromEvent , bufferTime , filter } from " rxjs " ; // listen to clicks on the whole document const clicks$ = fromEvent ( document . documentElement , " click " , // selecte only properties we need ( event ) => ({ type : event . type , time : new Date (), x : event . x , y : event . y }) ); clicks$ . pipe ( // flush events every 1 second bufferTime ( 1000 ), // move next only if there is at least one event filter (( events ) => events . length > 0 ) ) // process the batch . subscribe (( events ) => { fetch ( " /my-analytics " , { method : " POST " , headers : { " Content-Type " : " application/json " }, body : JSON . stringify ( events ) }); });

这里还有一个活生生的例子。

请注意，没有灵丹妙药，每种解决方案都有其缺点。
由于阻塞主线程，大量事件的批量处理可能会大大降低应用程序的性能，您应该不惜一切代价避免这种情况。 如果您希望处理大量数据，请考虑使用消息队列。 以BullMQ为例。

感谢您的阅读！

如果您可以分享一些在您的项目中应用事件批处理的示例，那就太好了。

Ivad Yves HABIMANA - Ivad Yves HABIMANA - Apr 13 4月13日



原文:

Vladimir Ivanenko

Posted on May 7

Roughly speaking event batching is accumulating events for some time to process them all at once later.

We can employ two well-known strategies or even combine them to understand when it's time to flush and process the bunch of events buffered:

  • when the number of events reaches a threshold;
  • using an interval — e.g. flush events every 10 seconds.

Why use it

Event batching could be beneficial for:

  • performance reasons, for example, to reduce the number of writes into persistent storage or to optimize the amount of data transmitted over a network;
  • aggregation — e.g. to group by link and count page visits.

How to

Implementing event batching in JavaScript with RxJS is a no-brainer.

Let's start with a Node example.

const EventEmitter = require('events');
const { fromEvent, bufferCount } = require('rxjs'); // I assume you already have an instance of EventEmitter in your app. 
// In case I'm wrong, let's create the one.
const eventEmitter = new EventEmitter(); // listen to an event called `something-good-happened`
fromEvent(eventEmitter, 'something-good-happened') // accumulate events .pipe( // and flush them every time it's number reaches 3 bufferCount(3), // let's log it tap(() => { console.log( `Great! The number of good things happened in a row reached ${events.length}. It's time to celebrate.` ); console.log(events); }) ) // process the batch .subscribe((events) => { const goodThingsByUser = {}; for (const event of events) { goodThingsByUser[event.userId] = (goodThingsByUser[event.userId] ?? 0) + 1; } // reportGoodThingsDone(goodThingsByUser); });

And of course, an example for a browser.

import { fromEvent, bufferTime, filter } from "rxjs"; // listen to clicks on the whole document
const clicks$ = fromEvent( document.documentElement, "click", // selecte only properties we need (event) => ({ type: event.type, time: new Date(), x: event.x, y: event.y })
); clicks$ .pipe( // flush events every 1 second bufferTime(1000), // move next only if there is at least one event filter((events) => events.length > 0) ) // process the batch .subscribe((events) => { fetch("/my-analytics", { method: "POST", headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" }, body: JSON.stringify(events) }); });

One more live example here.

Note, that there is no silver bullet and each solution has its drawbacks.
Bulk processing of a large bunch of events could dramatically worse the performance of your app because of blocking the main thread, which you should avoid at all costs. In case you expect to process lots of data consider using a message queue. Look at BullMQ for example.

Thank you for reading!

It would be great if you can share some examples of applying event batching in your projects.

Ivad Yves HABIMANA - Apr 13
