Items.Title: #05：保护静态 Web 应用程序 | #05: Securing Static Web Apps
Items.Description:
欢迎来到#30DaysOfSWA的Week 1, Day 5天！

到目前为止，我们已经学会了构建/部署一个基本的 Web 应用程序、添加一个无服务器 API 端点以及自定义应用程序行为和构建工作流的配置设置。 但是我们忘记了一件事——用户！ 为了为用户创造差异化的体验，我们需要方法来识别他们并了解他们在应用程序上下文中的角色。 然后，我们可以使用该信息来管理用户对应用程序功能的访问（基于角色的授权）以及应用程序对用户信息的访问（一旦经过身份验证）。

值得庆幸的是，Azure 静态 Web 应用程序让这一切变得相当轻松。 让我们看看如何。

我们将涵盖的内容

  • 我们如何保护 SWA？
  • 托管身份验证
  • 自定义身份验证
  • 管理用户角色
  • 基于角色的授权
  • 访问用户信息

关于作者

今天，Mitali Karmarkar 加入了我的行列，他是 Microsoft 的一名软件工程师，负责 Azure 应用服务和 Azure 静态 Web 应用产品。 打个招呼并在 Twitter 上关注她的@KarmarkarMitali ！

保护 SWA

保护静态 Web 应用程序意味着什么？ 这是三件事：

  • 了解用户身份- 有一种方法可以使用身份验证服务或提供者来确定用户的身份。
  • 了解用户角色- 了解用户在（使用应用程序）时所扮演的角色，并相应地定制体验。
  • 管理用户访问- 确保所有 SWA 组件都可以无缝访问身份验证信息，从而允许它们强制执行仅允许授权用户或角色访问资源的规则。

让我们深入了解解决这些需求的三个概念：身份验证、角色分配和基于角色的授权。

托管身份验证

预配置提供程序：Twitter、GitHub、Azure Active Directory

  • 预先分配的角色：匿名（访客）或认证（登录时）
  • 可定制的规则：为路由定义，在staticwebapps.config.json

    • 默认情况下，所有预配置的提供程序都处于活动状态，它们有自己的预定义 API 端点用于Login ，以及共享 API 端点用于Logout 。

    • 想要阻止特定的预配置提供商？ 只需在他们的登录路径中添加一个阻止规则，以确保不允许任何用户访问它。
    • 想要在登录尝试成功或失败后将用户发送到自定义页面？ 使用查询参数和路由规则来完成这项工作。

    这是默认的托管身份验证体验。

    自定义身份验证

    但是，如果您希望注册过程具有更大的灵活性（由预配置的提供程序使用）或想要添加一个新的（自定义提供程序）怎么办？
    这是自定义身份验证选项的用武之地，允许您覆盖默认值。

    为此，您可以配置一个或多个自定义提供程序。 您还可以配置支持OpenID Connect标准的自定义提供程序。

    通过配置至少一个自定义提供程序，您可以有效地禁用预配置的提供程序选项。 要配置自定义提供程序，请将配置信息添加到staticwebapps.config.json文件的auth部分。 这也是使用应用程序设置（环境变量）以保护隐私的方式存储潜在敏感配置数据的好地方。

    用户角色

    在这种情况下，您需要了解三个关键概念：

    • 每个静态 Web 应用程序用户都有一个或多个角色分配给他们。
    • 有 2 个内置角色（“匿名”和“经过身份验证”）
    • 定义自定义角色并通过邀请或按功能分配用户。

    让我们快速回顾一下。

    • 访问静态 Web 应用程序的所有用户都是匿名角色（访客），直到通过预配置或自定义提供程序进行身份验证。
    • 一旦通过身份验证，他们就具有经过身份验证的角色，但现在可以通过邀请（主动）或功能（反应）分配自定义角色
    • 可以使用用户的提供商特定的电子邮件地址从 Azure 门户配置和发送主动邀请。 用户现在单击邀请链接以使用自动分配的自定义角色登录。
    • 反应式函数将分配推迟到登录后，使用 Azure 函数在获得经过身份验证的用户详细信息时动态地向用户返回分配的角色（或多个角色）。

    后一种方法提供了更大的灵活性，允许您使用函数处理程序来调用另一个服务（如Microsoft Graph ）来为您做出角色分配决策。 阅读角色管理了解更多详细信息 - 包括更新角色分配或从角色中删除用户的步骤。

    基于角色的身份验证

    一旦用户被分配了一个角色，使用基于附加到每个route的allowedRoles属性的路由规则的内置支持，基于该分配管理用户访问变得容易。 例如，以下配置设置（在staticwebapps.config.json中）会将所有以/profile*开头的路由的访问权限限制为仅经过身份验证的用户。 ) )



    原文:

    Welcome to Week 1, Day 5 of #30DaysOfSWA!!

    So far, we've learnt to build/deploy a basic web app, add a serverless API endpoint and customize the configuration settings for both application behaviors and build workflows. But we forgot one thing - the user! To create differentiated experiences for users, we need ways to identify them and understand their role in the application context. We can then use that information to manage user access to application features (role-based authorization) as well as application access to user information (once authenticated).

    Thankfully, Azure Static Web Apps makes this fairly painless. Let's see how.

    What We'll Cover

    • How do we secure a SWA?
    • Managed authentication
    • Custom authentication
    • Managing user roles
    • Role-based authorization
    • Accessing user information

    About the Author(s)

    Today, I'm joined by Mitali Karmarkar, a Software Engineer at Microsoft working on the Azure App Service and Azure Static Web Apps products. Say hello and follow her @KarmarkarMitali on Twitter!

    Securing SWA

    What does it mean to secure a Static Web App? It's three things:

    • Knowing User Identity - Having a way to determine the identity of the user using an authentication service or provider.
    • Knowing User Roles - Understanding what persona the user is taking on when (using the application), and customizing the experience accordingly.
    • Managing User Access - Making sure the authenticaton information is seamlessly accessible to all SWA components, allowing them to enforce rules that allow only authorized users or roles to access resources.

    Let's dive into the three concepts that tackle these needs: Authentication, Role Assignment and Role-Based Authorization.

    Managed Auth

    Azure Static Web Apps comes with a default streamlined authentication experience that supports:

    • pre-configured providers: Twitter, GitHub, Azure Active Directory
    • pre-assigned roles: anonymous (guest) or authenticated (on login)
    • customizable rules: defined for routes, in staticwebapps.config.json

    All pre-configured providers are active by default, with their own pre-defined API endpoints for Login, and a shared API endpoint for Logout.

    • Want to block a specific pre-configured provider? Just add a blocking rule to their login route to ensure no user is allowed access to it.
    • Want to send users to custom pages after successful or failed login attempts? Use query parameters and route rules to make this work.

    This is the default managed authentication experience.

    Custom Auth

    But what if you want more flexibility with the registration process (used by a pre-configured provider) or want to add a new one (custom provider)?
    This is where custom authentication options come in, allowing you to override the defaults.

    You can configure one or more custom providers for this purpose. Azure Static Web Apps has built-in support for custom authentication using: Apple, Facebook, Google, Twitter, GitHub - and AAD! You can also configure custom providers that support OpenID Connect standard.

    By configuring at least one custom provider, you are effectively disabling pre-configured provider options. To configure custom providers, add config information to the auth section of the staticwebapps.config.json file. This is also a good place to use Application Settings (environment variables) to store potentially sensitive configuration data in a privacy-preserving way.

    Azure Static Web Apps makes custom registrations easy by providing standard route patterns for Login, Logout and User Profile requests, as well as redirect URLs for authentication callbacks (from the custom provider) on login/logout.

    User Roles

    There are three key concepts you need to know in this context:

    • Every Static Web Apps user has one or more roles assigned to them.
    • There are 2 built-in roles ("anonymous" and "authenticated")
    • Define custom roles & assign users by invitation or by-function.

    Let's review these quickly.

    • All users accessing a Static Web App are in an anonymous role (guest) until authenticated by a pre-configured or custom provider.
    • Once authenticated, they have an authenticated role but can now be assigned custom roles by invitation (proactive) or by function (reactive)
    • Proactive invitations can be configured and sent from the Azure Portal, using provider-specific email addresses for the user. Users now click on the invite link to login with that custom role assigned automatically.
    • Reactive functions defer assignment to post-login, using an Azure Function that dynamically returns an assigned role (or roles) to the user on being given the authenticated user details.

    The latter approach offers more flexibility, allowing you to use the function handler to invoke another service (like Microsoft Graph) to make the role-assignment decision for you. Read Role Management for more details - including steps to update role assignments or remove users from a role.

    Role-Based Auth

    Once the user has been assigned a role, managing user access based on that assignment becomes easy using the built-in support for routing rules based on the allowedRoles property attached to each route. For example, the following configuration setting (in staticwebapps.config.json) would restrict access to all routes that start with /profile* to only authenticated users. )

Items.Title: State of React Wrap-up | React 总结状态 | April 26, 2022 2022 年 4 月 26 日 | State of React Wrap-up | April 26, 2022
Items.Description:
在本次 State of React 活动中，主要话题集中在 React 18 版本上。 我们的小组成员对这个版本、它的最新功能和 React 工作组有很多想法。 我们还获得了有关 Redux 状态的最新信息，并听取了我们小组成员在 2022 React Miami 会议上的经历。

这是参加在线活动的主持人和小组成员的完整列表。

主机

  • 小组成员

    • 你可以在This Dot Media YouTube Channel上观看完整的State of React 事件。

      对话开始于小组成员分享他们对最新版本的看法以及为什么他们觉得它有点反气候。 经过多年的努力和期待，最终版本并没有像之前的 React 版本那样在社区中产生那么多噪音。 一些小组成员认为，很多关于最新变化的博客文章和对话都发生在几个月前。 这可能有助于这次安静的发布。

      多年来，React 18 工作组在与社区沟通新版本的进展方面做得很好。 他们还尝试将大量社区反馈融入到这个最新版本中。 有人向小组提出了一个问题，也许人们已经厌倦了 React，这就是为什么它是反气候的。 但小组成员认为，这个安静的发布意味着 React 正在成为 JavaScript 生态系统中的一个标准。 他们还提出了一个很好的观点，即许多开发人员在正式发布日期之前就已经在使用一些新功能。

      小组成员如何看待 React 18 的新特性？

      一位小组成员表示有兴趣尝试新的useTransition挂钩，它允许您将某些状态指定为较低的优先级。 但他们也指出，在他们当前的项目中并不急需整合一些新功能。 有时你在你的代码库中工作很舒服，使用已经存在的现有钩子很好。 这可能会成为第一次学习 React 的新开发人员会推动使用这些新功能的情况。

      这引发了关于 React 18 如何帮助解决库作者遇到的许多问题的讨论。 例如，新的并发悬念和转换 API 将使加载器指标更容易。 但是还有一个问题提到了一些小组成员的兴趣。 小组成员对它的易用性以及如何改进可访问性感到兴奋。 他们也很感激名称从useOpaqueIdentifier更改为useId 。

      但小组成员确实相信这个小组为未来的主要版本定下了基调。 如前所述，React 工作组在与社区合作方面做得很好，并创造了从 React 17 到 React 18 的平稳过渡。

      在迁移到 React 18 之前，开发人员需要学习哪些新功能？

      提到的第一个特性是新的根 API。 在 React 17 中，这就是渲染 App 组件的方式。

       import { render } from " react-dom " ; import App from " App " ; render ( < App tab = " home " /> , document . getElementById ( " app " ));

      但在 React 18 中，您将使用新的createRoot API。

       import { createRoot } from " react-dom/client " ; import App from " App " ; const root = createRoot ( document . getElementById ( " app " )); root . render ( < App tab = " home " /> );

      提到的另一个功能是flushSync ，它允许您选择退出自动批处理。

      在谈话的这一点上，我们过渡到了关于 Redux 状态的简短 powerpoint 演示文稿。
      以下是该演示文稿中提到的关键点。

      • 这个新版本适用于 React 16.8、17、18 和 React Native
      • 人们现在应该开始迁移到 React 18 还是有等待期？

      在这部分对话中，小组成员讨论了 React 18 支持的内容。例如，如果您的应用程序使用 Enzyme 进行测试，那么 React 18 将不支持它。这将需要您重写所有测试以适应这个最新版本。 在迁移之前，开发人员需要了解这个新版本支持的内容。 小组成员还指出，社区使用 React 18 需要几个月的时间才能发现潜在问题。

      再次举行面对面的会议是什么感觉？

      我认为 Mark Erikson 对 2022 React Miami 会议的总结是最好的。

      嗯，这对我有用。 但说真的，Ben 和 Kathleen 都进行了会谈，并在会议上度过了愉快的时光。 普遍的共识是，很高兴再次亲自与社区联系，即使有时很奇怪。

      是否有针对 React 18 的新课程或文章？

      在React 工作组 GitHub 存储库中有很多精彩的讨论。 许多作者能够从这些讨论中创建出色的博客文章。

      您可以在此Meta 案例研究中了解更多相关信息。

      由于 React 是无主见的，因此应该有一个地方可以让开发人员了解与 React 一起工作的常用技术。

      幸运的是，This Dot Labs 创建了一个工具来解决这个问题。 可访问性很重要

      在最后的谈话中，有很多关于新的 Rect 18 功能将如何改进可访问性的讨论。 小组成员还认为，所有开发人员都应该更加关心创建构建良好的可访问应用程序，并花时间了解最佳可访问性实践。

      结论

      这是与一群伟大的小组成员进行的一次令人难以置信的对话，我强烈建议您观看视频。 你可以在This Dot Media YouTube Channel上观看完整的State of React 事件。

      如需 React、Angular、Vue、Web 组件、GraphQL、Node、Bazel 或 Polymer 方面的专家架构指导、培训或咨询，请访问thisdot.co



原文:

In this State of React event, the main topic focused on the React 18 release. Our panelists had a lot of thoughts on the release, its latest features, and the React working group. We also got an update on the state of Redux, and heard about our panelists' experiences at the 2022 React Miami conference.

Here is a complete list of the hosts and panelists that participated in the online event.

Hosts

  • Dustin Goodman, Engineering Manager, This Dot Labs, @dustinsgoodman
  • Dane Grant, Senior Software Engineer, This Dot Labs, @danecando

Panelists

  • Jen Luker, Senior Staff Frontend Engineer, Nav, Inc., @knitcodemonkey
  • Ben Ilegbodu, Frontend Architect, Stitch Fix, @benmvp
  • Romello Goodman, Educator at MICA & Senior Engineer, Shopify, @mellogood
  • Kathleen McMahon, Senior Design Systems Engineer, Northwestern Mutual, @resource11
  • Chantastic, DX Engineer, Chromatic, @chantastic
  • Mark Erikson, Senior Front-End Engineer, Replay, @acemarke

You can watch the full State of React event on the This Dot Media YouTube Channel.

React 18 is finally here!!!

The conversation got started with the panelists sharing their thoughts on the latest release and why they felt like it was a little bit anti-climatic. After years of hard work and anticipation, the final release didn't generate as much noise in the community as previous versions of React. Some of the panelists believe that a lot of the blog posts and conversations about the latest changes happened months ago. This might have contributed to a quiet release this time around.

There was also praise for the React 18 working group which did a great job of communicating with the community over the years about progress of the new version. They also tried to incorporate a lot of the community feedback into this latest release. The hard work and care of the React 18 working group also contributed to the quiet and stable release of this latest version.

Does the anti-climatic release of React 18 signal that people are tired of React?

There was a question posed to the group that maybe people were tired of React and that is why it was anti-climatic. But the panelists believe that this quiet release means that React is becoming more of a standard in the JavaScript ecosystem. They also brought up the good point that a lot of developers have already been working with some of the new features before the official release date.

What do the panelists think about the new features of React 18?

One of the panelists expressed interest in trying out the new useTransition hook which allows you to specify some states as a lower priority. But they also made the point that there wasn't an immediate need in their current projects to incorporate some of the newer features. Sometimes you are comfortable working in your codebase and it is fine to use the existing hooks that are already there. It might become a situation where new developers learning React for the first time will push for the use of these new features.

This led to conversation about how React 18 helped solve a lot of problems that library authors were experiencing. For example, the new concurrent suspense and transition APIs will make it easier for loader indicators. But there was another hook mentioned that peaked the interest of some of the panelists. The useId hook generates a unique random ID that is consistent in server side and client side rendering. The panelists were excited about how easy it was to use and how it would improve on accessibility. They were also grateful that the name was changed from useOpaqueIdentifier to useId.

Will the React working group continue?

There are no current plans for the React working group, and it has been relatively quiet since the release. But the panelists do believe that this group set the tone for future major releases. As mentioned earlier, the React working group did a great job working with the community and creating a smooth transition from React 17 to React 18.

What are some of the new features that developers need to learn before migrating to React 18?

The first feature that was mentioned was the new root API. In React 17, this is how you would render your App component.

import { render } from "react-dom";
import App from "App";
render(<App tab="home" />, document.getElementById("app"));

But with React 18, you would use the new createRoot API.

import { createRoot } from "react-dom/client";
import App from "App"; const root = createRoot(document.getElementById("app"));
root.render(<App tab="home" />);

Another feature that was mentioned was flushSync which allows you to opt out of automatic batching.

The State of Redux with Mark Erikson

At this point of the conversation, we transitioned into a short powerpoint presentation on the state of Redux.
Here are the key points mentioned in that presentation.

  • React-Redux v8.0 is now live.
  • Redux JS/TS templates for Create React App have been updated.
  • This new version works with React 16.8, 17, 18 and React Native
  • React-Redux v8.0 has been converted to TypeScript
  • Redux Toolkit 1.8 has a new "listener" side effects middleware which allows you to do powerful async/await workflows.
  • Redux 4.2.0 has officially marked createStore as deprecated

Should people start migrating over to React 18 now or is there a waiting period?

During this part of the conversation panelists talked about what is supported by React 18. For example, if your application uses Enzyme for testing, then it will not be supported by React 18. This would require you to rewrite all of your tests just to accommodate this latest version. Developers need to understand what is supported by this new version before migrating over. The panelists also pointed out that it will take a few months of the community working with React 18 before potential issues come to light.

What was React Miami like? What was it like to have in person conferences again?

I think Mark Erikson had the best quote to sum up the 2022 React Miami conference.

"Warm, fun, awesome, tiring, weird"

Well, that works for me. :)

But in all seriousness, both Ben and Kathleen gave talks and enjoyed their time at the conference. The general consensus was that it was nice to connect with the community again in person, even if it was weird at times.

Are there any new exciting courses or articles coming out for React 18?

There was a lot of a great discussions in the React working group GitHub repository. A lot of authors were able to create great blog posts out of those discussions.

There was also a quick shout out to Dustin and Dane for their involvement with the new React beta site. You can learn more about that in this Meta case study.

The downside of React being unopinionated

Mark brought up the issue he has seen with React developers not knowing what to use for styling, state management and build tools. Since React is unopinionated, there should be a place where developers can learn about the common technologies that work well with React.

Luckily for us, This Dot Labs has created a tool to tackle this issue. react.framework.dev is a community driven list of resources for React.

Accessibility matters

In the closing conversation, there was a lot of discussion on how the new Rect 18 features will improve on accessibility. The panelists also felt like all developers should care more about creating well built accessible applications and take the time to learn about best accessibility practices.

Conclusion

This was an incredible conversation with a great group of panelists and I would highly suggest you watch the video. You can watch the full State of React event on the This Dot Media YouTube Channel.

This Dot Labs is a modern web consultancy focused on helping companies realize their digital transformation efforts. For expert architectural guidance, training, or consulting in React, Angular, Vue, Web Components, GraphQL, Node, Bazel, or Polymer, visit thisdot.co
Items.Author: codergirl1991

Items.Title: 12 个提供信息和启发的 Web 开发播客 | 12 Web Development Podcasts That Inform and Inspire
Items.Description:
播客可以成为娱乐和教育的重要来源。 但随着播客的数量庞大，有时很难知道该听什么。

在本文中，我将分享我最喜欢的播客，以收听讨论最新的网络技术和职业。

现代网络

现代网络播客通过采访领先的行业专业人士来探索最新的网络开发。 过去的嘉宾包括 Evan You（Vue.js 的创建者）、Jem Young（Netflix 的工程经理）和 Mark Thompson（Google 的开发人员关系工程师）。

主持人都是This Dot Labs的软件架构师，他们非常出色地为客人提出了发人深省的问题。

我最喜欢的一集是 Jesse Tomchak 采访 Jem Young并讨论了他作为工程经理的角色以及他迄今为止所面临的挑战。

代码新手

如果您是新开发人员并希望了解有关该行业的更多信息，那么CodeNewbie就是适合您的播客。 部分嘉宾包括丹尼·汤普森、阿里特·阿马纳、卡西迪·威廉姆斯、昆西·拉森和安吉·琼斯。

我最喜欢的一集的标题是freeCodeCamp 如何随着 Quincy Larson 的发展而演变。 这一集记录了 Quincy 的 freeCodeCamp 之旅，并涵盖了该组织的未来发展。

变更日志：软件开发，开源

他们的一些客人包括 Feross Aboukhadijeh、Annie Sexton 和传奇计算机科学家 Brian Kernighan。

我最喜欢的一集是对 Brian Kernighan 的采访，他分享了他对软件在 50 多年职业生涯中如何发展的看法。

如果您有兴趣了解 JavaScript 的所有内容，那么JS Party就是您开始的地方。 您将了解流行的 Web 技术、性能、开源项目等方面的最新发展。

每周，他们都有不同的主持人，包括 Jerod Santo、Feross Aboukhadijeh、Kevin Ball 和 Amelia Wattenberger。 他们之前的一些客人包括 Kent Dodds、Zach Leatherman 和 Jen Looper。

我最喜欢的一集名为Remix 有助于弥合网络鸿沟，Kent Dodds 谈到了这个新框架以及为什么它是 Web 生态系统的一个很好的补充。

前端欢乐时光

如果您正在寻找讨论前端 Web 开发的所有内容的悠闲播客，请收听前端欢乐时光。

每周，来自 Netflix、Twitch 和 Atlassian 的软件工程师小组将邀请客人在与饮料的随意交谈中讨论他们在前端开发方面的经验。 之前的嘉宾包括 Sumana Mohan、Joe King、Nazanin Delam 和 Dan DiGangi。

我最喜欢的一集名为《 在拥挤的酒吧中脱颖而出》，主持人邀请泰勒·德塞恩和乔诺·格雷森讨论如何在就业市场上脱颖而出。

开发者茶

开发人员 Tea制作了关于职业发展的简短有影响力的剧集。 反应播客

如果您了解 React 并想了解最新进展，那么React Podcast适合您。

之前的嘉宾包括 Shawn Swyx Wang、Cassidy Williams 和 Kent C. Dodds。

我最喜欢的一集名为Kent C. Dodds on Epic React ，他们在其中讨论了他广受欢迎的 React 课程以及它将如何帮助您升级为开发人员。

主持人小组包括 Aimee Knight、AJ O'Neal、Charles Max Wood、Dan Shappir 和 Steve Edwards。

我最喜欢的一集是关于Sam Sycamore 从景观建筑工人转变为全职开发人员的故事。

句法

我最喜欢听的一集是JavaScript × STUMP'D集，两位主持人试图回答 JavaScript 面试问题。

我最喜欢听的一集是名为“Web 开发的黑暗面”的一集，它让我们了解了这个行业不那么令人愉快的一面。

虚拟咖啡播客

每周，Dan Ott 和 Bekah Hawrot Weigel 都会采访一位社区成员，讨论他们的科技之旅。

我最喜欢的一集名为Yolanda Haynes - 闯入科技：这需要时间，讲述了她进入 Web 开发的旅程以及她在此过程中学到的经验教训。

在The Virtual Coffee Podcast上有很多鼓舞人心的故事可以听，这些故事既有趣又有教育意义。

免费代码营

他们的播客中有很多关于职业改变者如何过渡到 Web 开发的故事。

我最喜欢的一集名为“一位前音乐老师如何自学编码并在 GitHub 找到工作”。

结论

我希望您喜欢我列出的 12 个播客列表，它们将为您的编码之旅提供信息和启发！ 我喜欢在工作期间收听这些播客，我希望你也喜欢。

以下是我期待在未来探索并受到开发者社区好评的更多播客。

如需 React、Angular、Vue、Web 组件、GraphQL、Node、Bazel 或 Polymer 方面的专家架构指导、培训或咨询，请访问thisdot.co



原文:

Podcasts can be a great source of entertainment and education. But with the sheer volume of podcasts out there, sometimes it is hard to know what to listen to.

In this article, I will share my favorite podcasts to listen to that discuss the latest in web technologies and careers.

Modern Web

The Modern Web podcast explores the latest in web development by interviewing leading industry professionals. Past guests have included Evan You (Creator of Vue.js), Jem Young (Engineering Manager at Netflix) and Mark Thompson (Developer Relations Engineer at Google).

The hosts are all Software Architects at This Dot Labs and they do a great job coming up with thought provoking questions for the guests.

My favorite episode is when Jesse Tomchak interviewed Jem Young and discussed his role as an engineering manager and the challenges he has faced so far.

CodeNewbie

If you are new developer and looking to learn more about the industry, then CodeNewbie is the podcast for you. Saron Yitbarek (host) and the rest of the team do a great job selecting a diverse range of topics and make it accessible to all developers.

Some of the guests have included Danny Thompson, Arit Amana, Cassidy Williams, Quincy Larson and Angie Jones.

My favorite episode is titled How freeCodeCamp has evolved over time with Quincy Larson. This episode chronicles Quincy's journey with freeCodeCamp and covers future developments for the organization.

The Changelog: Software Development, Open Source

The Changelog has over 400 episodes of interviews with leading industry professionals discussing everything from AI, to open source, UI/UX, engineering management, and more.

Adam Stacoviak and Jerod Santo do a great job creating engaging conversations with their guests and keeping it educational for the audience. Some of their guests have included Feross Aboukhadijeh, Annie Sexton and legendary computer scientist Brian Kernighan.

My favorite episode would have to be the interview on Brian Kernighan as he shares his thoughts on how software has evolved over his 50+ year career.

JS Party: JavaScript, CSS, Web Development

If you are interested in learning about all things JavaScript, then JS Party is the place to start. You will learn about the latest developments in popular web technologies, performance, open source projects and more.

Each week, they have a different set of hosts that include Jerod Santo, Feross Aboukhadijeh, Kevin Ball, and Amelia Wattenberger. Some of their previous guests have included Kent Dodds, Zach Leatherman, and Jen Looper.

My favorite episode is titled Remix helps bridge the network chasm where Kent Dodds talks about this new framework and why it is a good addition to the web ecosystem.

Front End Happy Hour

If you are looking for a laid back podcast discussing all things frontend web development, then take a listen to Front End Happy Hour.

Each week, a panel of Software Engineers from Netflix, Twitch, & Atlassian will invite guests to discuss their experiences with frontend development over a casual conversation with drinks. Previous guests have included Sumana Mohan, Joe King, Nazanin Delam, and Dan DiGangi.

My favorite episode is titled Standing out in a crowded bar where the hosts bring on Taylor Desseyn and Jono Grayson to discuss ways to stand out in the job market.

Developer Tea

Developer Tea produces short impactful episodes on career advancement. Jonathan Cutrell (host) covers a variety of topics including effectively working with management, staying motivated, and building better work relationships with your team.

A good episode to listen to would be Swizec Teller's two part episode on the Senior Engineering Mindset.

React Podcast

If you learning about React and want to hear about the latest developments, then the React Podcast is for you.

Michael Chan (host) interviews leading professionals about their thoughts on the industry and all things React development. Previous guests have included Shawn Swyx Wang, Cassidy Williams and Kent C. Dodds.

My favorite episode is titled Kent C. Dodds on Epic React where they discuss his popular React course and how it will help you level up as a developer.

JavaScript Jabber

JavaScript Jabber covers all things JavaScript including frontend architecture, Web3, React, D3 and more. The panel of hosts include Aimee Knight, AJ O'Neal, Charles Max Wood, Dan Shappir, and Steve Edwards.

My favorite episode is about Sam Sycamore's story on transitioning from landscape construction worker to full time developer.

Syntax

The Syntax podcast explores all this web related including the latest in Gatsby, AWS, Web3, Remix and more. Wes Bos and Scott Tolinski (hosts) do a great job creating fun and educational content for their listeners.

My favorite episode to listen to would be the JavaScript × STUMP’D episode where the two hosts try to answer JavaScript interview questions.

HTML All The Things

HTML All The Things cover all things web development, web design and small business. Matt Lawrence and Mike Karan (hosts) discuss a variety of topics including entrepreneurship, how to handle stress, Web3, web performance, and more.

My favorite episode to listen to is the one titled The Dark Side of Web Development which provides a look into the not so pleasant side of this industry.

Virtual Coffee Podcast

Virtual Coffee is a community of developers at all stages of their software journey who collaborate and support each other along the way. Each week, Dan Ott and Bekah Hawrot Weigel interview one of the community members to discuss their journey into tech.

My favorite episode is titled Yolanda Haynes - Breaking into Tech: It Takes Time which follows her journey into web development and the lessons she learned along the way.

There are plenty of inspiring stories to listen to on The Virtual Coffee Podcast that are both entertaining and educational.

freeCodeCamp

freeCodeCamp is an educational non-profit company with a mission to provide free programming education to everyone regardless of age, race or socioeconomic background. Their podcast features a lot of stories on how career changers made the transition into web development.

My favorite episode is titled How a former music teacher taught herself to code and landed a job at GitHub.

Conclusion

I hope you enjoyed my list of 12 podcasts that will inform and inspire your coding journey! I enjoy listening to these podcasts during work and I hope you do too.

Here are some more podcasts I am looking forward to exploring in the future and have been well received by the developer community.

This Dot Labs is a modern web consultancy focused on helping companies realize their digital transformation efforts. For expert architectural guidance, training, or consulting in React, Angular, Vue, Web Components, GraphQL, Node, Bazel, or Polymer, visit thisdot.co

Items.Title: remix-domains 如何改进 Remix 项目的（已经很棒的）DX？ | How remix-domains improves the (already awesome) DX of Remix projects?
Items.Description:
在Seasoned ，我们绝对爱上了Remix 。 我们已经使用它几个月了，我们真的认为它是一个很棒的工具。
但与我们将使用的任何东西一样，我们发现了一些我们认为开箱即用方法不足的领域。

在这篇文章中，我将讨论其中的一些问题，并展示我们如何使用我们的新库： remix-domains来解决这些问题。

水暖

一旦任何项目开始变得有点严肃，对结构化架构的需求就会开始出现。 在 Remix 的动作和加载器的几乎每个示例中，我们都可以看到一些我们称之为管道的样板。

例如，看看 Jokes App 的这段代码的操作。 很多事情正在发生！ 这通常是 Remix Apps 的外观，将很多责任转嫁给控制器。

让我们来看看常见的 loader/action 的职责：

  • 正如您可能已经猜到的那样，我们将加载器和操作视为控制器。 我们认为控制器应该只“说 HTTP”。 在这种心态下，我们会将上面的列表重构为仅步骤A 和 D 。 步骤B 和 C就是我们所说的业务逻辑，即使项目独一无二的代码。

    在 Seasoned，我们喜欢将此代码分成定义良好/经过测试/类型化的域。

    那么我们如何将业务逻辑与remix-domains解耦呢？

    首先，我们将为用户输入和环境数据编写 Zod 模式：

     // app/domains/jokes.ts const jokeSchema = z . object ({ name : z . string (). min ( 2 , `That joke's name is too short` ), content : z . string (). min ( 10 , ' That joke is too short ' ), }) const userIdSchema = z . string (). nonempty ()

    然后我们将使用这些模式编写业务逻辑：

     // app/domains/jokes.ts import { makeDomainFunction } from ' remix-domains ' // ... const createJoke = makeDomainFunction ( jokeSchema , userIdSchema ) (( fields , jokesterId ) => db . joke . create ({ data : { ... fields , jokesterId } }) )

    最后我们将编写控制器的代码：

     // app/routes/jokes/new.tsx import { inputFromForm } from ' remix-domains ' import type { ErrorResult } from ' remix-domains ' // ... export const action : ActionFunction = async ({ request }) => { const result = await createJoke ( await inputFromForm ( request ), await getUserId ( request ), ) if ( ! result . success ) { return json < ErrorResult > ( result , 400 ) } return redirect ( `/jokes/ ${ result . data . id } ?redirectTo=/jokes/new` ) }

    现在，让我们重写这个 loader 。 我们从域开始：

     // app/domains/jokes.ts const getRandomJoke = makeDomainFunction ( z . null (), userIdSchema ) ( async ( _i , jokesterId ) => { const count = await db . joke . count () const skip = Math . floor ( Math . random () * count ) return db . joke . findMany ({ take : 1 , skip , where : { jokesterId } }) })

    然后，加载器：

     // app/routes/jokes/index.tsx import type { UnpackData } from ' remix-domains ' // ... type LoaderData = UnpackData < typeof getRandomJoke > export const loader : LoaderFunction = async ({ request }) => { const result = await getRandomJoke ( null , await getUserId ( request ) ) if ( ! result . success ) { throw new Response ( ' No jokes to be found! ' , { status : 404 }) } return json < LoaderData > ( result . data ) }

    您是否看到这些控制器上出现了一种模式？

    如果您想查看使用remix-domains实现的完整 Jokes App，请查看此 PR diff ！。

    保持模式

    由于控制器做了这么多，很难保持一种标准的方式来构建成功和失败的响应。

    我们应该在控制器中添加try/catch吗？
    我们将如何返回输入错误以及如何将它们与实际运行时或服务错误区分开来？

    您也不需要使用try/catch块，我们实际上可以在我们的域函数中抛出错误，与 Remix 内部使用的相同模式，因此您只能编写域函数的快乐路径并throw错误以确保类型安全：

     const getJoke = makeDomainFunction ( z . object ({ id : z . string (). nonempty () }), userIdSchema ) ( async ({ id }, jokesterId ) => { const joke = await db . joke . findOne ({ where : { id , jokesterId } }) if ( ! joke ) throw new Error ( ' Joke not found ' ) return joke })

    在上面的域函数上，如果成功，响应将如下所示：

     const result = { success : true , data : { id : 1 , name : ' Joke name ' , content : ' Joke content ' }, inputErrors : [], environmentErrors : [], errors : [], }

    否则它看起来像这样：

     const result = { success : false , inputErrors : [], environmentErrors : [], errors : [{ message : ' Joke not found ' }], }

    现在我们从我们的动作/加载器中减少了样板并找到了一种架构模式，很容易开始在它们之上创建我们自己的小抽象。

     // app/lib/index.ts function queryResponse < T > ( result : T ) { if ( ! response . success ) throw new Response ( ' Not found ' , { status : 404 }) return json < T > ( result . data ) } // app/routes/jokes/$id.tsx import type { UnpackResult } from ' remix-domains ' type LoaderData = UnpackData < typeof getJoke > export const loader : LoaderFunction = async ({ params }) => { return queryResponse < LoaderData > ( await getJoke ( params )) }

    测试

    现在假设您需要彻底测试该原始代码。

    目前，没有一种简单的方法可以做到这一点，而无需模拟路由器 API。 解决方案通常在于端到端测试。

    不过，我们想在我们的业务逻辑中进行单元和集成测试。 现在我们的域函数只是接收数据和返回数据的函数，我们可以很容易地编写它们：

     // Soon we'll be writing about how we set up our test database. // For the sake of brevity, pretend there's already a DB with jokes describe ( ' getRandomJoke ' , () => { it ( ' returns a joke for the given userId ' , async () => { const { user , jokes } = await setup () const result = await getRandomJoke ( null , user . id ) if ( ! result . success ) throw new Error ( ' No jokes to be found! ' ) expect ( jokes . map (({ id }) => id )). toInclude ( result . data . id ) }) })

    如果您曾经尝试在您的 Remix 路线上进行此类测试，您可能会对您刚刚看到的内容感到满意。

    从表单解析结构化数据

    好的，这不是 Remix 限制，而是FormData API 的“限制”。
    解析表单中的结构化数据通常很有用，例如，当您有嵌套表单或可重复字段时。

    可以说它不适用于复杂的形式。

    通过结构化数据，我的意思是从这个表单制作FormData ：

     <form method= "post" > <input name= "numbers[]" value= "1" /> <input name= "numbers[]" value= "2" /> <input name= "person[0][email]" value= "john@doe.com" /> <input name= "person[0][password]" value= "1234" /> <button type= "submit" > Submit </button> </form>

    被解释为：

     { "numbers" : [ "1" , "2" ], "person" : [{ "email" : "john@doe.com" , "password" : "1234" }]
}

    好吧， remix-domains利用qs为您使用inputFromForm进行转换：

     import { inputFromForm } from ' remix-domains ' const result = await myDomainFunction ( await inputFromForm ( request ))

    该库公开了其他实用程序来完成此类工作。

    端到端类型安全和组成

    关于 Remix（和 NextJS）的最大抱怨之一是缺乏端到端的类型安全性。
    必须手动维护类型很无聊并且容易出错。 我们想要一个和tRPC一样好的体验，现在我们的域函数已经知道你域的 I/O，我们处于与 tRPC 相同的情况， 正如其作者所说：

    至于输出类型——tRPC 信任从解析器推断出的返回类型，这是合理的 IMO。 这些类型主要来自提供可信类型的来源（Prisma 查询等）。

    顺便说一句：Colin 也是Zod的作者，还有很多不错的项目，我们对他的项目的喜爱程度怎么夸都不为过。

    使用remix-domains时，您不需要手动编写类型，因为它们是从域函数中推断出来的。 以下 GIF 显示了此工作流程的 DX：

    作品

    当我们开始从事这个项目时，我们没有意识到我们会实现如此好的类型安全性，也没有计划通过始终将域函数的返回表示为Promise<Result<MyData>>来创建完美的算术级数。

    因此，当我们遇到在一个加载器中处理多个域而不改变控制器架构的问题时，答案就摆在我们面前：创建函数来组合多个域函数，从而产生……一个域函数！

    到目前为止，我们已经创建了3 个函数，使我们能够像这样编写代码：

     import { all , map , pipe } from ' remix-domains ' import type { UnpackData } from ' remix-domains ' import { a , b , c , d , e } from ' ./my-domain-functions ' // Transform the successful data, ex: // { success: true, data: "foo" } => { success: true, data: true } const x = map ( a , Boolean ) // Create a domain function that is a sequence of Domain Functions // where the output of one is the input of the next const y = pipe ( x , b , c ) // Create a domain function that will run all the given domain functions // in parallel with `Promise.all` const getData = all ( d , e , y ) type LoaderData = UnpackData < typeof getData > export const loader : LoaderFunction = async ({ params }) => { return queryResponse < LoaderData > ( await getData ( params )) } export default function Component () { const [ dataForD , dataForE , dataForY ] = useLoaderData < LoaderData > () // ... }

    所有这些逻辑和我们加载程序的样板都没有改变！

    另一个 GIF 展示了用于合成的 DX：

    结论

    我们对这个项目感到非常兴奋，我们希望你会喜欢它。
    我们一直在对其进行实战测试，迫不及待地想看到社区的反馈。

    如果您对上面的示例感到迷茫，请查看remix-domains的文档和示例。

    如果它可以帮助更多团队构建他们的项目，我们将会欣喜若狂，我们期待您的反馈/贡献。



原文:

At Seasoned we are absolutely in love with Remix. We have been working with it for a few months and we really think it is a great tool.
But as with anything we'd use, we've found a few areas where we think the out-of-the-box approach falls short.

In this post, I'm gonna talk about some of them and show how we are solving those problems with our new library: remix-domains.

Plumbing

As soon as any project starts to get a little serious a demand for a structured architecture starts to emerge. On almost every example of Remix's actions and loaders out there, we can see some boilerplate we call plumbing.

For instance, take a look at the action on this chunk of code of the Jokes App. A lot is going on! And this is usually how Remix Apps look, relaying a lot of responsibility to the controllers.

Let's put the common loader/action's responsibilities in perspective:

  • A) Extracting data out of the request, be it from the query string, the body, the url, request headers, etc.
  • B) Assert that this data is in the right format, maybe doing some data munging after that.
  • C) Doing some side effects, such as inserting data into the database, sending emails, etc.
  • D) Returning a different Response depending on the result of previous steps.
  • E) There's also a need to manually maintain the types of your Responses aligned with what the UI expects, we'll talk more about it later in this post.

As you've probably guessed already, we think about loaders and actions as controllers. And we think controllers should only "speak HTTP". In that mindset, we'd refactor the list above to only steps A and D. Steps B and C are what we call Business logic, the code which makes the project unique.

And at Seasoned we like to separate this code into well defined/tested/typed domains.

So how would we decouple that business logic with remix-domains?

First, we'd write Zod schemas for both the user input and the environment data:

// app/domains/jokes.ts
const jokeSchema = z.object({ name: z.string().min(2, `That joke's name is too short`), content: z.string().min(10, 'That joke is too short'),
}) const userIdSchema = z.string().nonempty()

Then we'd write the business logic using those schemas:

// app/domains/jokes.ts
import { makeDomainFunction } from 'remix-domains'
// ... const createJoke = makeDomainFunction(jokeSchema, userIdSchema) ((fields, jokesterId) => db.joke.create({ data: { ...fields, jokesterId } }) )

And lastly we'd write the controller's code:

// app/routes/jokes/new.tsx
import { inputFromForm } from 'remix-domains'
import type { ErrorResult } from 'remix-domains'
// ... export const action: ActionFunction = async ({ request }) => { const result = await createJoke( await inputFromForm(request), await getUserId(request), ) if (!result.success) { return json<ErrorResult>(result, 400) } return redirect(`/jokes/${result.data.id}?redirectTo=/jokes/new`)
}

Now, let's rewrite this loader. We start with the domain:

// app/domains/jokes.ts
const getRandomJoke = makeDomainFunction(z.null(), userIdSchema) (async (_i, jokesterId) => { const count = await db.joke.count() const skip = Math.floor(Math.random() * count) return db.joke.findMany({ take: 1, skip, where: { jokesterId } }) })

Then, the loader:

// app/routes/jokes/index.tsx
import type { UnpackData } from 'remix-domains'
// ... type LoaderData = UnpackData<typeof getRandomJoke>
export const loader: LoaderFunction = async ({ request }) => { const result = await getRandomJoke( null, await getUserId(request) ) if (!result.success) { throw new Response('No jokes to be found!', { status: 404 }) } return json<LoaderData>(result.data)
}

Do you see a pattern emerging on those controllers?

If you want to see the full Jokes App implemented with remix-domains check this PR diff!.

Keeping a Pattern

With controllers doing so much, it is harder to keep a standard way to structure your successful and failed responses.

Should we be adding try/catch in our controllers?
How are we going to return input errors and how to differ 'em from actual runtime or service errors?

remix-domains does that for you, by having a structured way to present data and errors, you can be sure the responses will always be consistent. You also don't need to work with try/catch blocks, we can actually just throw errors in our domain functions, the same pattern Remix uses internally, so you can only write the happy path of your domain functions and throw errors to ensure type safety:

const getJoke = makeDomainFunction(z.object({ id: z.string().nonempty() }), userIdSchema) (async ({ id }, jokesterId) => { const joke = await db.joke.findOne({ where: { id, jokesterId } }) if (!joke) throw new Error('Joke not found') return joke })

On the domain function above, if successful the response will look like this:

const result = { success: true, data: { id: 1, name: 'Joke name', content: 'Joke content' }, inputErrors: [], environmentErrors: [], errors: [],
}

Otherwise it'll look like this:

const result = { success: false, inputErrors: [], environmentErrors: [], errors: [{ message: 'Joke not found' }],
}

Now that we reduced the boilerplate out of our actions/loaders and found an architectural pattern, it is easy to start creating our own little abstractions on top of them.

// app/lib/index.ts
function queryResponse<T>(result: T) { if (!response.success) throw new Response('Not found', { status: 404 }) return json<T>(result.data)
} // app/routes/jokes/$id.tsx
import type { UnpackResult } from 'remix-domains' type LoaderData = UnpackData<typeof getJoke>
export const loader: LoaderFunction = async ({ params }) => { return queryResponse<LoaderData>(await getJoke(params))
}

Testing

Now imagine you need to thoroughly test that original code.

Currently, there's no easy way to do it, without kind of mocking the Router API. The solution usually lies in E2E testing.

We'd like to do unit and integration tests in our business logic, though. Now that our domain functions are just functions that receive data and return data, we can easily write them:

// Soon we'll be writing about how we set up our test database.
// For the sake of brevity, pretend there's already a DB with jokes
describe('getRandomJoke', () => { it('returns a joke for the given userId', async () => { const { user, jokes } = await setup() const result = await getRandomJoke(null, user.id) if (!result.success) throw new Error('No jokes to be found!') expect(jokes.map(({ id }) => id)).toInclude(result.data.id) })
})

If you ever tried to do such sort of testing on your Remix routes you are probably happy about what you've just seen.

Parsing structured data from Forms

Ok, this is not a Remix limitation but rather a "limitation" of the FormData API.
It is often useful to parse structured data from forms, for example, when you have nested forms or repeatable fields.

FormData can only work with flat structures and we need to know the structure of the data beforehand to know if we should call formData.get('myField') or formData.getAll('myField'). It arguably does not scale for complex forms.

By structured data I mean making the FormData from this form:

<form method="post"> <input name="numbers[]" value="1" /> <input name="numbers[]" value="2" /> <input name="person[0][email]" value="john@doe.com" /> <input name="person[0][password]" value="1234" /> <button type="submit"> Submit </button>
</form>

Be interpreted as this:

{ "numbers": ["1", "2"], "person": [{ "email": "john@doe.com", "password": "1234" }]
}

Well, remix-domains leverages qs to do that conversion for you with inputFromForm:

import { inputFromForm } from 'remix-domains' const result = await myDomainFunction(await inputFromForm(request))

The library exposes other utilities for doing that sort of job.

End-to-end Type Safety and composition

One of the biggest complaints about Remix (and NextJS) is the lack of end-to-end type safety.
Having to maintain the types by hand is boring and prone to errors. We wanted an experience as good as tRPC and now that our domain functions have knowledge of your domain's I/O we are in the same situation as tRPC as stated by its author:

As for output types—tRPC trusts the return type inferred from your resolver which is reasonable IMO. These types largely originate from sources (Prisma queries, etc) that provide trustworthy types.

BTW: Colin is also the author of Zod and a bunch of nice projects, we can't overstate how much we like his projects.

When working with remix-domains, you don't need to be writing types by hand as they are infered out of the domain functions. The following GIF shows the DX of this workflow:

Composition

When we started working on this project, we didn't realize we'd achieve such a good type safety nor did we planned to create a perfect arithmetic progression by always expressing the return of our Domain Functions as Promise<Result<MyData>>.

So when we were confronted with the problem of working with multiple domains in a single loader without changing our controller's architecture, the answer was right in front of us: creating functions to compose multiple Domain Functions, resulting in... a Domain Function!

So far we've created 3 functions enabling us to code like this:

import { all, map, pipe } from 'remix-domains'
import type { UnpackData } from 'remix-domains'
import { a, b, c, d, e } from './my-domain-functions' // Transform the successful data, ex:
// { success: true, data: "foo" } => { success: true, data: true }
const x = map(a, Boolean)
// Create a domain function that is a sequence of Domain Functions
// where the output of one is the input of the next
const y = pipe(x, b, c)
// Create a domain function that will run all the given domain functions
// in parallel with `Promise.all`
const getData = all(d, e, y) type LoaderData = UnpackData<typeof getData>
export const loader: LoaderFunction = async ({ params }) => { return queryResponse<LoaderData>(await getData(params))
} export default function Component() { const [ dataForD, dataForE, dataForY ] = useLoaderData<LoaderData>() // ...
}

All that logic and our loader's boilerplate is untouched!

This other GIF showcases the DX for compositions:

Conclusion

We are pretty excited about this project and we hope you'll like it.
We've been battle-testing it and can't wait to see the feedback of the community.

If you felt lost about the examples above, check out the documentation and examples of remix-domains.

We are gonna be over the moon if it helps more teams to structure their projects and we are looking forward to your feedback/contributions.
Items.Author: gugaguichard

Items.Title: Rockset 如何处理重复数据删除 | How Rockset Handles Data Deduplication
Items.Description:
作者：Tyler Denton，Rockset 销售工程师

分布式数据系统有两个主要问题。 第二个是乱序消息，第一个是重复消息，第三个是一个错误，第一个是重复消息。

这个笑话启发 Rockset 通过我们称为重复数据删除的过程来应对数据重复问题。

随着数据系统变得越来越复杂以及堆栈中系统数量的增加，重复数据删除变得更具挑战性。 这是因为重复可以通过多种方式发生。 这篇博客文章讨论了数据重复，它如何困扰采用实时分析的团队，以及 Rockset 提供的用于解决重复问题的重复数据删除解决方案。 每当将另一个分布式数据系统添加到堆栈中时，组织就会厌倦其工程团队的运营税。

重复问题

在分布式系统中，消息在许多工作人员之间来回传递，并且消息生成两次或更多次是很常见的。 系统可能会创建重复的消息，因为：

  • 未发送确认。
  • 消息在发送之前已被复制。
  • 消息确认在超时后出现。
  • 消息被乱序传递，必须重新发送。

当消息到达数据库管理系统时，可以多次接收到具有相同信息的消息。 因此，您的系统必须确保不会创建重复记录。 重复记录的成本可能很高，并且会不必要地占用内存。 这些重复的消息必须合并为一条消息。

重复数据删除解决方案

在 Rockset 之前，存在三种通用的重复数据删除方法：

  1. 在它发生之前停止复制。
  2. 在 ETL 作业期间停止复制。
  3. 在查询时停止重复。

重复数据删除历史

但是，如果问题发生在 Kafka 的上游，他们的系统会将这些消息视为非重复消息，并以不同的时间戳传递重复的消息。 因此，恰好一次语义并不总能解决重复问题，并且会对下游工作负载产生负面影响。

在它发生之前停止复制

一些平台试图在复制发生之前停止它。 这似乎很理想，但这种方法需要进行困难且成本高昂的工作来确定重复的位置和原因。

重复通常由以下任一原因引起：

  • 交换机或路由器。
  • 失败的消费者或工人。
  • 流量太大。
  • 对数据包来说太小的窗口大小。

注意：请记住，这不是一个详尽的列表。

这种重复数据删除方法需要深入了解系统网络以及硬件和框架。 即使对于全栈开发人员来说，了解 OSI 模型所有层的复杂性及其在公司中的实施也是非常罕见的。 任何规模的组织中的数据存储、对数据管道的访问、数据转换和应用程序内部都超出了单个人的范围。 因此，组织中有专门的职称。 对重复消息的所有位置进行故障排除和识别的能力需要深入的知识，这对于个人甚至跨职能团队来说都是不合理的。 尽管成本和专业知识要求非常高，但这种方法提供了最大的回报。

在 ETL 作业期间停止复制

流处理 ETL 作业是另一种重复数据删除方法。 这涉及数据流使用期间的重复数据删除。 消费渠道可能包括创建压缩主题和/或使用通用批处理工具（例如，Fivetran、Airflow 和 Matillian）引入 ETL 作业。

为了使用流处理 ETL 作业方法使重复数据删除有效，您必须确保 ETL 作业在整个系统中运行。 由于数据复制可以应用于分布式系统中的任何地方，因此确保架构在消息传递的所有地方都进行重复数据删除至关重要。

流处理器可以有一个活动的处理窗口（在特定时间打开），可以检测和压缩重复消息，并且可以重新排序无序消息。 如果在处理窗口之外接收到消息，则可以复制消息。 此外，必须维护这些流处理器，并且可能会占用大量计算资源和操作开销。

注意：在活动处理窗口之外收到的消息可以复制。 我们不建议单独使用此方法解决重复数据删除问题。

在查询时停止复制

另一种去重方法是尝试在查询时解决它。 但是，这会增加查询的复杂性，这是有风险的，因为可能会生成查询错误。

例如，如果您的解决方案使用时间戳跟踪消息，并且重复消息延迟一秒（而不是 50 毫秒），则重复消息上的时间戳将与您的查询语法不匹配，从而导致引发错误。

该系统将团队从前面介绍的许多繁琐的重复数据删除选项中解放出来。

每个文档都有一个称为_id的唯一标识符，其作用类似于主键。 用户可以使用基于 SQL 的转换在摄取时（例如在更新期间）指定此标识符。 当具有相同_id的新文档到达时，重复的消息将合并到现有记录中。 这为用户提供了一个简单的解决重复问题的方法。

当您将数据导入 Rockset 时，您可以使用以下 SQL 转换构建自己的复杂_id键：

  • 识别单个键。
  • 识别复合键。
  • 从多个键中提取数据。

只要您在要更新或插入的文档中指定带有_id或标识_id的消息，传入的重复消息将被删除重复并合并到一个文档中。

其他分析数据库将数据存储在固定数据结构中，这需要压缩、重新分片和重新平衡。 任何时候对现有数据进行更改，都需要对存储结构进行大修。 许多数据系统都有活动窗口，以避免对存储结构进行大修。 因此，如果您将_id映射到活动数据库之外的记录，则该记录将失败。 相比之下，Rockset 用户具有很大的数据流动性，可以随时更新 Rockset 中的任何记录。

虽然我们已经谈到了其他系统中重复数据删除的运营挑战，但还有一个计算支出元素。 在查询时尝试重复数据删除，或使用 ETL 作业对于许多用例而言计算成本可能很高。

这是一个匿名用户的故事，我曾与我密切合作过他们的实时分析用例。

客户背景

一个客户有大量的数据更改，在他们的数据仓库中创建了重复的条目。 尽管客户只想要数据的当前状态，但每次数据库更改都会产生一条新记录。

如果客户想要将此数据放入无法映射_id的数据仓库中，客户将不得不循环浏览存储在其数据库中的多个事件。 这包括运行基本查询，然后运行其他事件查询以获取最新的值状态。 这个过程在计算上是极其昂贵和耗时的。

因此客户只需要查询最新的状态。 得益于此功能，Rockset 使该客户能够减少所需的计算以及查询处理时间——有效地交付亚秒级查询。



原文:

by Tyler Denton, Sales Engineer, Rockset

There are two major problems with distributed data systems. The second is out-of-order messages, the first is duplicate messages, the third is off-by-one errors, and the first is duplicate messages.

This joke inspired Rockset to confront the data duplication issue through a process we call deduplication.

As data systems become more complex and the number of systems in a stack increases, data deduplication becomes more challenging. That's because duplication can occur in a multitude of ways. This blog post discusses data duplication, how it plagues teams adopting real-time analytics, and the deduplication solutions Rockset provides to resolve the duplication issue. Whenever another distributed data system is added to the stack, organizations become weary of the operational tax on their engineering team.

Rockset addresses the issue of data duplication in a simple way, and helps to free teams of the complexities of deduplication, which includes untangling where duplication is occurring, setting up and managing extract transform load (ETL) jobs, and attempting to solve duplication at a query time.

The Duplication Problem

In distributed systems, messages are passed back and forth between many workers, and it’s common for messages to be generated two or more times. A system may create a duplicate message because:

  • A confirmation was not sent.
  • The message was replicated before it was sent.
  • The message confirmation comes after a timeout.
  • Messages are delivered out of order and must be resent.

The message can be received multiple times with the same information by the time it arrives at a database management system. Therefore, your system must ensure that duplicate records aren’t created. Duplicate records can be costly and take up memory unnecessarily. These duplicated messages must be consolidated into a single message.

Deduplication Solutions

Before Rockset, there were three general deduplication methods:

  1. Stop duplication before it happens.
  2. Stop duplication during ETL jobs.
  3. Stop duplication at query time.

Deduplication History

Kafka was one of the first systems to create a solution for duplication. Kafka guarantees that a message is delivered once and only once. However, if the problem occurs upstream from Kafka, their system will see these messages as non-duplicates and deliver the duplicate messages with different timestamps. Therefore, exactly once semantics do not always solve duplication issues and can negatively impact downstream workloads.

Stop Duplication Before it Happens

Some platforms attempt to stop duplication before it happens. This seems ideal, but this method requires difficult and costly work to identify the location and causes of the duplication.

Duplication is commonly caused by any of the following:

  • A switch or router.
  • A failing consumer or worker.
  • A problem with gRPC connections.
  • Too much traffic.
  • A window size that is too small for packets.

Note: Keep in mind this is not an exhaustive list.

This deduplication approach requires in-depth knowledge of the system network, as well as the hardware and framework(s). It is very rare, even for a full-stack developer, to understand the intricacies of all the layers of the OSI model and its implementation at a company. The data storage, access to data pipelines, data transformation, and application internals in an organization of any substantial size are all beyond the scope of a single individual. As a result, there are specialized job titles in organizations. The ability to troubleshoot and identify all locations for duplicated messages requires in-depth knowledge that is simply unreasonable for an individual to have, or even a cross-functional team. Although the cost and expertise requirements are very high, this approach offers the greatest reward.

Stop Duplication During ETL Jobs

Stream-processing ETL jobs is another deduplication method. ETL jobs come with additional overhead to manage, require additional computing costs, are potential failure points with added complexity, and introduce latency to a system potentially needing high throughput. This involves deduplication during data stream consumption. The consumption outlets might include creating a compacted topic and/or introducing an ETL job with a common batch processing tool (e.g., Fivetran, Airflow, and Matillian).

In order for deduplication to be effective using the stream-processing ETL jobs method, you must ensure the ETL jobs run throughout your system. Since data duplication can apply anywhere in a distributed system, ensuring architectures deduplicate in all places messages are passed is paramount.

Stream processors can have an active processing window (open for a specific time) where duplicate messages can be detected and compacted, and out-of-order messages can be reordered. Messages can be duplicated if they are received outside the processing window. Furthermore, these stream processors must be maintained and can take considerable compute resources and operational overhead.

Note: Messages received outside of the active processing window can be duplicated. We do not recommend solving deduplication issues using this method alone.

Stop Duplication at Query Time

Another deduplication method is to attempt to solve it at query time. However, this increases the complexity of your query, which is risky because query errors could be generated.

For example, if your solution tracks messages using timestamps, and the duplicate messages are delayed by one second (instead of 50 milliseconds), the timestamp on the duplicate messages will not match your query syntax causing an error to be thrown.

How Rockset Solves Duplication

Rockset solves the duplication problem through unique SQL-based transformations at ingest time.

Rockset is a Mutable Database

Rockset is a mutable database and allows for duplicate messages to be merged at ingest time. This system frees teams from the many cumbersome deduplication options covered earlier.

Each document has a unique identifier called _id that acts like a primary key. Users can specify this identifier at ingest time (e.g. during updates) using SQL-based transformations. When a new document arrives with the same _id, the duplicate message merges into the existing record. This offers users a simple solution to the duplication problem.

When you bring data into Rockset, you can build your own complex _id key using SQL transformations that:

  • Identify a single key.
  • Identify a composite key.
  • Extract data from multiple keys.

Rockset is fully mutable without an active window. As long as you specify messages with _id or identify _id within the document you are updating or inserting, incoming duplicate messages will be deduplicated and merged together into a single document.

Rockset Enables Data Mobility

Other analytics databases store data in fixed data structures, which require compaction, resharding and rebalancing. Any time there is a change to existing data, a major overhaul of the storage structure is required. Many data systems have active windows to avoid overhauls to the storage structure. As a result, if you map _id to a record outside the active database, that record will fail. In contrast, Rockset users have a lot of data mobility and can update any record in Rockset at any time.

A Customer Win With Rockset

While we've spoken about the operational challenges with data deduplication in other systems, there's also a compute-spend element. Attempting deduplication at query time, or using ETL jobs can be computationally expensive for many use cases.

Rockset can handle data changes, and it supports inserts, updates and deletes that benefit end users. Here’s an anonymous story of one of the users that I’ve worked closely with on their real-time analytics use case.

Customer Background

A customer had a massive amount of data changes that created duplicate entries within their data warehouse. Every database change resulted in a new record, although the customer only wanted the current state of the data.

If the customer wanted to put this data into a data warehouse that cannot map _id, the customer would’ve had to cycle through the multiple events stored in their database. This includes running a base query followed by additional event queries to get to the latest value state. This process is extremely computationally expensive and time consuming.

Rockset's Solution

Rockset provided a more efficient deduplication solution to their problem. Rockset maps _id so only the latest states of all records are stored, and all incoming events are deduplicated. Therefore the customer only needed to query the latest state. Thanks to this functionality, Rockset enabled this customer to reduce both the compute required, as well as the query processing time — efficiently delivering sub-second queries.
Items.Author: shawn

Items.Title: 提示：使用本地 Vim 配置编辑服务器上的文件 | Tip: Edit Files on a Server Using Your Local Vim Config
Items.Description:
安德雷斯库科

发表于最近我发现您可以使用 scp 协议使用 vim 编辑托管在服务器上的文件，并保留本地.vimrc / init.nvim配置！

在本教程中，我将编辑托管在 EC2 实例上的文件，为此您需要：

  • 用于授权的.per或.cer文件
  • 您通常用于通过 ssh 连接到服务器的 IP 地址

就是这样！ 让我们首先设置权限。

  1. 在您的终端上，运行vim ~/.ssh/config以创建或修改现有配置文件
  2. 复制并粘贴以下内容，将其替换为您的服务器值
Host your-ec2-instance-name Hostname 2.89.32.932 User ubuntu IdentityFile /Users/myname/path/to/your-ec2-instance-name.cer PasswordAuthentication no
  1. 运行nvim scp://your-ec2-instance-name/home/ubuntu/my-file.txt

就是这样！ 系统可能会提示您授权主机，在这种情况下您需要输入yes 。



原文:

andrescuco

Posted on May 7

Recently I found out that you can edit files that are hosted on a server with vim using the scp protocol, and keep your local .vimrc / init.nvim config!

On this tutorial I'll be editing files that are hosted on an EC2 instance, to do this you'll need:

  • A .per or .cer file for authorization
  • The ip address that you'd normally use to connect to your server through ssh

And that's it! Let's first setup the permissions.

  1. On your terminal, run vim ~/.ssh/config to either create or modify an existing config file
  2. Copy and paste the following, replacing it with your server's value 
Host your-ec2-instance-name
Hostname 2.89.32.932
User ubuntu
IdentityFile /Users/myname/path/to/your-ec2-instance-name.cer
PasswordAuthentication no
  1. Run nvim scp://your-ec2-instance-name/home/ubuntu/my-file.txt

And that's it! You might be prompted with a message to authorize the host in which case you'll need to type yes.
Items.Author: andrescuco

Items.Title: 预测肯塔基德比 2022 比赛的结果 | Predicting outcomes of Kentucky Derby 2022 race
Items.Description:
马克西姆·鲍伯

发表于肯塔基赛马是每年在美国肯塔基州路易斯维尔举行的赛马比赛。 我从我的一位同事那里得知了这场比赛。 他一直在做一个辅助项目，分析过去的比赛，以预测即将到来的赛马的结果。 我对赛马一无所知，并认为做同样的项目会很有趣。 我会做出预测，看看它们是否准确。 我还将学习如何使用 DS 在现实世界中获利。

预测

根据我的分析，我发现两名候选人很可能进入前三名。

马培训师骑师
充电托德·A·普莱彻路易斯·赛斯
简化安东尼奥·萨诺何塞·奥尔蒂斯

为什么？

主要是因为这个。 在骑师和培训师的社交网络之上运行社区检测算法后，我发现了这一点。

数据集中有明显的社区集群。 在我的同事建议找出有多少百分比的均值落入集群后，结果发现 2015-2021 年发生的所有胜利中有 50% 落入红色集群。

在查明是否有来自该获胜集群的驯马师参加当前比赛后，我发现有 2 位驯马师参加，但他们有 4 匹马，因此有 4 种选择可供选择。 然后我通过找出哪些骑师以前赢过，将我的范围缩小到两个（在我的笔记本中你可以看到为什么做出这样的假设是合理的）。

为什么不使用机器学习？

我不确定这个问题是否真的是“随机的”。 我有一种直觉，它类似于一个老男孩网络，所以通过进行社区分析，我可能会更好地获得优势。

为什么我认为这是一个老男孩网络？ 嗯，主要是因为鲍勃巴弗特有给他的马下药的历史。 他一直被取消参加赛马比赛的资格，直到 2023 年。巧合的是，鲍勃·巴弗 (Bob Baffer) 属于这个赢得 50% 的时间的红色集群。 这可能是一个巧合，但知道环法自行车赛丑闻和俄罗斯在奥运会中使用兴奋剂，我不会对肯塔基德比比赛中可能存在这样的事情感到惊讶。

资源

  • 笔记本探索
  • 穆里洛·梅内塞斯·门东萨 - 朱利安·朗格朗-兰伯特 - Feb 11 2月11日



原文:

Maksim Bober

Posted on May 7

Kentucky Derby is a horse race that takes place each year in Louisville, Kentucky, United States. I learned about this race from one of my coworkers. He has been doing a side project analyzing past races to predict the outcome of the upcoming horse race. I didn't know anything about horse racing and thought it would be fun to do the same project. I would make predictions and see if they are accurate or not. I would also learn a lesson about how to profit in the real world using DS.

Predictions

As a result of my analysis I found that two candidates are likely to finish in the top 3.

Horse Trainer Jockey
Charge It Todd A. Pletcher Luis Saez
Simplification Antonio Sano Jose Ortiz

Why?

Mostly because of this. After running a community detection algorithm on top of the social network of jockey and trainer I found this.

There are clear community clusters in the dataset. After suggestion of my coworker to find out what percentage of the means falls into the cluster it turned out that 50% of all wins that happened from 2015-2021 fall into the red cluster.

After finding out if there are trainers from this winning cluster participate in the current race I found out that there are 2 trainers participating but they have 4 horses so there are 4 choices to choose from. Then I norrowed down my scope to two by finding out which jockeys have won before (in my notebook you can see why it's reasonable to make such an assumption).

Why not to use ML?

I'm not sure that this problem is truly "random". I have an intuition that it runs simillar to an old boy network so by doing a community analysis I might get a better shot at getting an edge.

Why do I think that it's an old boy network? Well primarily because of Bob Baffert which has a history of drugging his horses. He has been disqualified from participating in the horse competition until 2023. Coincidently, Bob Baffer falls into this red cluster that wins 50% of the time. It might be a coincidence but knowing about Tour De France scandal and Doping in Olympic games by Russia, I would not be surprised that something like this might exist in Kentucky Derby competition.

Resources

  • Notebook Exploration
  • Wolfram Community Post

Murilo Menezes Mendonça - Feb 3

Julien Lengrand-Lambert - Feb 11
Items.Author: bobrinik

Items.Title: 走出 Apple 生态系统的故事。 有些。 | Story about getting out of Apple ecosystem. Somewhat.
Items.Description:
讲故事的时间。 在我还年轻的时候，我想我是被一部老电影《黑客》（我相信是在 90 年代左右上映的）所打动的，这使我成为了开源社区的坚定信徒。 这确实影响了我使用的软件的决定。 如果我没记错的话 - 从 6.06 版开始使用 Ubuntu，称为 Dapper Drake。 好吧，事实上我能够运行几个带有 compiz 和双盒的 Lineage (MMORPG) 客户端，而在 Wine 上的麻烦比在 Windows XP 上要少。 更不用说在他还是个少年并住在立陶宛的时候收到一张 Ubuntu 安装 CD 的感觉。 这在当时是一件大事——但这不是重点。

快进十年，我正在伦敦学习软件工程。 我记得当时我刚买了一台华硕 N53SV（当时的顶级笔记本电脑），当然我正在运行 Linux（Arch BTW）并使用所有开源软件。 当然减去一些游戏。 但后来发生了一些事情，我转向黑暗面并开始使用...... OS X！ 令我惊讶的是，我爱上了它，以至于后来我什至得到了一部 iPhone。 一切通过 iCloud 运作的方式简直就是魔法。 没有cron作业来同步笔记、提醒。 什么都没有 - 只需创建它，它就会在我所有的设备上同步。 获得了我的第一个软件工程工作，一家公司也在使用 Apple 设备，所以一切正常。 活在梦里。

然后我搬到另一家公司，在那里待了几年，并决定用公司预算购买一台新笔记本电脑（升级我的个人 MB Pro - 带有蹩脚键盘的版本）。 这就是我的搜索开始的地方。

获得一个新的 MBP 不是一个选择——因为 M1 指日可待，我已经在考虑哪些方法不适合我的日常工作。 主要选择是让带有 Ryzen 和 32GB 内存的联想 T14 来喂鲸鱼（码头工人 - 看着你）。 同时，我读到 WSL(2) 非常适合它，所以给自己买了一个旧的 ThinkPad 并给了它一个公平的震动。 令我惊讶的是 - 我的开发机器所需的一切...... badum tss......刚刚工作！ 除此之外 - 想尝试带触摸屏的笔记本电脑，笔记本电脑搜索变得有点激烈。 长话短说 - 由于芯片短缺、硬件所需的功能等，我给自己买了一台戴尔 XPS 13 2 合 1 7390。对机器很满意，一切都很好。 或者我是这么想的。

愚蠢的我忘记了其中一个关键的、有点与工作相关的软件——笔记和提醒（嗯，是待办事项列表的一部分）。 在这一点上，我失去了所有的便利，开始寻找跨平台软件。 作为一个直接的跨平台解决方案，我开始使用 Microsoft Todo 和 Notes 应用程序（因为这些应用程序的 iOS 应用程序相当不错）。 与此同时，我再次换了工作，公司使用...苹果设备！ 所以现在我需要一些真正跨平台的东西来跨 Windows（个人笔记本电脑）、OS X（工作笔记本电脑）、iOS 设备（个人电话）和我的 Linux 塔式 PC 工作。 在尝试了多个应用程序、电子版本甚至渐进式网络应用程序之后，我终于开始使用Notion和Todoist的免费版本。 整个过程大概花了4个月的时间。 是的，我就是这样的人。 可以让客户跨越我使用的所有平台。 如果 Todoist 在免费版本中允许“提醒”功能，那就太好了——但我可以没有它。 现在，3 个月过去了，我可以自信地说，我不会错过我的纯苹果生态系统。 如果只是 - 所有这些情况都提醒我尝试新事物，因为我喜欢的应用程序中有一些功能（例如 Notion 中的降价支持！）。 我什至考虑将我的 iPhone 11 Pro 升级到三星 Fold 4（当它发布时）。 故事的寓意 - 不要被使用某些东西的便利性所蒙蔽，如果这不会以任何方式限制你，请三思而后行，因为寻找替代品至少可以说是痛苦的！

我知道它是加密的，现在一切都需要花费，但仍然如此！



原文:

Story time. When I was a quite a bit younger, I guess I was moved by an old movie ”Hackers” (I believe released in the 90-ies or so) which made me a strong believer in the Open Source community. That really impacted the decisions on software I was using. If I remember correctly - started using Ubuntu from version 6.06 called Dapper Drake. Well that and the fact I was able to run a couple of Lineage (MMORPG) clients with compiz and dual box it with less hassle over the Wine than on Windows XP. Not to mention the feeling of receiving an Ubuntu install CD in the post while still being a teenager and living in Lithuania. That was a big deal back then - but that is not the point here.

Fast forward a decade and I am studying Software Engineering in London. I remember at the time I just bought an Asus N53SV (top of the line laptop at the time) and of course I was running Linux (Arch BTW) and using all of the open source software for everything. Minus some games of course. But then something happened and I turned to the dark side and started using... OS X! To my surprise, I was in love with it, to the point where later I even got an iPhone. And the way everything worked through iCloud was just magic. No cron jobs to sync notes, reminders. Nothing - just create and it is all synced across all of my devices. Got my first Software Engineering gig and a company was using Apple devices too, so everything just worked. Living the dream.

Then I moved to another company, been there for a few years and decided to use company budget for a new laptop (to upgrade my personal MB Pro - edition with the crappy keyboard). And that is where my search began.

To get a new MBP was not an option - since M1 was just around the corner and I was already considering what will not work to do my daily job. Primary option was to get Lenovo T14s with Ryzen and 32GB of Ram to feed the whale (docker - looking at you there). At the same time I read about WSL(2) being really good for what it is, so got myself an old ThinkPad and gave it a fair shake. To my surprise - everything what I needed for my development machine... badum tss... just worked! As well as that - wanted to try a laptop with touch screen and the laptop search got a bit intense. Long story short - due to chip shortages, required functions for hardware, etc I landed myself a Dell XPS 13 2-in-1 7390. Happy with the machine and all great. Or so I thought.

Silly me forgot one of the key, somewhat work related software pieces - notes and reminders (well, todo list part of it). At this point I was feeling lost missing all my convenience and the hunt for cross platform software began. As an immediate cross platform solution landed myself on using Microsoft Todo and Notes apps (since iOS apps for those are fairly decent ones). In the meantime, I changed job once again and company uses... Apple devices! So now I need something truly cross platform to work across Windows (personal laptop), OS X (work laptop), iOS device (personal phone) and my Linux powered tower PC. After trying multiple apps, electron versions and even progressive web apps I finally landed myself on using free versions of Notion and Todoist. The whole process took probably good 4 months. Yes I am that kind of perso. Both Notion and Todoinst apps have all the features I need/use and are trully cross platform. It is possible to get clients accross all paltforms I use. It would be great if Todoist would allow the “reminders” functionality on a free version - but I can live without it. Now, 3 months in and I can confidently say that I don’t miss my apple-only ecosystem. If only - all this situation reminded me to try new things as there are features on apps I love (e.g. markdown support in Notion!). It got to the point where I even consider upgrading my iPhone 11 Pro to Samsung Fold 4 (when it gets released). Moral of the story - don’t get blinded by the convenience of using something and think twice if that does not limit you in any way, as the search for replacement can be painful to say the least!

P.S. Really loved Obsidian notes app but $8 a month for sync functionality is expensive IMHO. I understand it is encrypted and everything costs these days but still!
Items.Author: epetkus

Items.Title: 使用 Twitter API v2 了解围绕 Tweet 的整个对话 | Understanding the entire conversation around a Tweet with the Twitter API v2
Items.Description:
有兴趣研究围绕某个主题的对话的研究人员经常使用搜索端点来获取有关某个主题的推文。 从搜索端点返回的这些推文中的每一个都可能在对它们的回复中以及在引用它们的推文中发生重要的后续对话，这可能会提供额外的信息以更好地理解整个对话。 在本教程中，您将学习如何获取推文的整个对话。

第一步：搜索关于某个主题的推文

在第一步中，您可以使用搜索端点搜索包含特定关键字并满足特定条件的主题的推文。 目前，Twitter API v2 中有 2 个搜索端点可用：

  • 搜索端点要求开发人员指定搜索查询以返回满足特定条件的推文。 或者，您可以指定您想要推文的时间段。

    默认情况下，每个请求您将获得 10 条推文（推文 ID 和推文文本）。 根据请求，使用 max_results 参数，您可以获得：

    如果你想要额外的推文，你可以使用带有 next_token 的分页。

    下面是从TwitterDev 帐户搜索推文的示例，使用 Python 中的最近搜索端点，使用Tweepy 包。

    注意：您首先需要注册一个开发者帐户并拥有您的不记名令牌以在下面的代码示例中使用。 按照本教程中的步骤获取您的不记名令牌。 此外，您需要在 Python 中安装 Tweepy 包才能运行下面的代码示例。 按照本指南中的说明安装 Tweepy。

     import tweepy client = tweepy . Client ( bearer_token = 'REPLACE_ME' ) # Replace with your own search query query = 'from:TwitterDev -is:retweet' # Replace the limit=1000 with the maximum number of Tweets you want for tweet in tweepy . Paginator ( client . search_recent_tweets , query = query , tweet_fields = [ 'context_annotations' , 'created_at' ], max_results = 100 ). flatten ( limit = 1000 ): print ( tweet . id )

    一旦您获得了关于您选择的主题的推文，您就可以获得每条推文的所有回复。

    第二步：对于推文，获取所有回复

    您可以使用搜索端点中可用的 conversation_id 参数获取对推文的所有回复。 此对话 ID 的值是原始推文的推文 ID。 下面的示例显示如何获取 ID 为 1503863303709286407 的推文的所有回复

    import tweepy client = tweepy . Client ( bearer_token = 'REPLACE_ME' ) # Replace with your own search query query = 'conversation_id:1503863303709286407 -is:retweet' # Replace the limit=1000 with the maximum number of Tweets you want for tweet in tweepy . Paginator ( client . search_recent_tweets , query = query , tweet_fields = [ 'context_annotations' , 'created_at' ], max_results = 100 ). flatten ( limit = 1000 ): print ( tweet . id )

    第三步：对于一条推文，获取其所有引用推文

    要获取推文的引用推文，您可以使用引用推文查找端点。 您可以将推文 ID 传递给它，并将排除参数设置为转推。 这将为您提供推文的所有原始引用推文。 下面的示例显示了如何获取 ID 为 1503863303709286407 的推文的所有引用推文。

     import tweepy client = tweepy . Client ( bearer_token = 'REPLACE_ME' ) # Replace the limit=1000 with the maximum number of Tweets you want for tweet in tweepy . Paginator ( client . get_quote_tweets , id = 1503863303709286407 , max_results = 100 , exclude = 'retweets' ). flatten ( limit = 1000 ): print ( tweet . id )

    就是这样。 您现在拥有一条推文的整个对话，包括所有回复和引用推文。 如果您对本教程有任何问题或反馈，请在Twitter 上与我联系。

    资源



原文:

The Twitter API allows developers and researchers to get Twitter data programmatically. Researchers interested in studying the conversation around a topic often use the search endpoints to get Tweets on a topic. Each of these Tweets returned from the search endpoints may have important follow-up conversations happening in replies to them, and in the Tweets quoting them, which may provide additional information to better understand the entire conversation. In this tutorial, you will learn how to get the entire conversation for a Tweet.

Step one: Search for Tweets about a topic

In the first step, you can search for Tweets on a topic, containing certain keywords and meeting certain criteria using the search endpoints. Currently, there are 2 search endpoints available in the Twitter API v2:

  • Recent search, available to all developers and gives Tweets from the last 7 days
  • Full-archive search, currently only available to academic researchers and gives Tweets from the entire archive of public Tweets

The search endpoints require developers to specify a search query to return Tweets meeting a certain criteria. Optionally, you can specify the time period from which you want the Tweets for.

By default, you will get 10 Tweets (Tweet ID and Tweet text) per request. Per request, using the max_results parameter, you can get upto:

If you want additional Tweets, you can use pagination with the next_token.

Below is an example of searching for Tweets from the TwitterDev account, using the recent search endpoint in Python using the Tweepy package.

Note: You will first need to sign up for a developer account and have your bearer token to use in the code samples below. Follow the steps in this tutorial to get your bearer token. Also, you will need to install the Tweepy package in Python to run the code sample below. Follow the instructions in this guide to install Tweepy.

import tweepy client = tweepy.Client(bearer_token='REPLACE_ME') # Replace with your own search query
query = 'from:TwitterDev -is:retweet' # Replace the limit=1000 with the maximum number of Tweets you want
for tweet in tweepy.Paginator(client.search_recent_tweets, query=query, tweet_fields=['context_annotations', 'created_at'], max_results=100).flatten(limit=1000): print(tweet.id)

Once you have the Tweets on the topic of your choice, for each Tweet you can get all its replies.

Step two: For a Tweet, get all replies to it

You can get all replies to a Tweet using the conversation_id parameter available in the search endpoints. The value for this conversation_id is the Tweet ID of the original Tweet. The example below shows how to get all replies for the Tweet with ID 1503863303709286407

import tweepy client = tweepy.Client(bearer_token='REPLACE_ME') # Replace with your own search query
query = 'conversation_id:1503863303709286407 -is:retweet' # Replace the limit=1000 with the maximum number of Tweets you want
for tweet in tweepy.Paginator(client.search_recent_tweets, query=query, tweet_fields=['context_annotations', 'created_at'], max_results=100).flatten(limit=1000): print(tweet.id)

Step three: For a Tweet, get all its Quote Tweets

To get Quote Tweets for a Tweet, you can use the Quote Tweets lookup endpoint. You can pass it the Tweet ID and set the exclude parameter to retweet. This will give you all original Quote Tweets of a Tweet. The example below shows how to get all Quote Tweets for the Tweet with ID 1503863303709286407.

import tweepy client = tweepy.Client(bearer_token='REPLACE_ME') # Replace the limit=1000 with the maximum number of Tweets you want
for tweet in tweepy.Paginator(client.get_quote_tweets, id=1503863303709286407, max_results=100, exclude='retweets').flatten(limit=1000): print(tweet.id)

That’s it. You now have the entire conversation for a Tweet including all replies and quote Tweets. The conversation_id and the quote Tweets lookup endpoints make it really easy for developers and researchers to understand the entire conversation around a Tweet. If you have any questions or feedback about this tutorial, please reach out to me on Twitter.

Items.Author: suhemparack

Items.Title: 在 Rails 中使用序列化器 | Using Serializers in Rails
Items.Description:
序列化器可用于为单个模型自定义 JSON 数据。 但是，它们也可用于处理多个模型之间的关联。 要使用具有一对多关联的序列化程序，请将 belongs_to 宏添加到相应的序列化程序。 示例：有三个模型，episode--<出场>--guests。 返回出场的 JSON 数据时，我想包含剧集和嘉宾的数据。 由于模型中已经建立了关系，我可以在相应的序列化程序中包含相同的has_one :episode和has_one :guest关系。 因此，在返回外观的 JSON 数据时，会包含剧集和客串的数据。


这同样可以用于多对多关联。 示例：我希望 JSON 数据包含客人以及剧集的数据。 在对应的剧集序列化器中包含has_many :guests关系将返回 JSON 数据以及剧集和来宾。


最能帮助我理解这些概念的是看到它们在行动中的作用。 练习编写不同的序列化程序以查看返回的 JSON 数据。



原文:

Serializers can be used to customize JSON data for a single model. However, they can also be used to handle associations between multiple models. The has_many and belongs_to Active Record macros can assist.

To use a serializer with a one-to-many association, add the belongs_to macro to the corresponding serializer. Example: there are three models, episode--< appearances >--guests. When the JSON data for appearances is returned, I want to include the data for episode and guest. Since the relationships have been established in the model, I can include the same has_one :episode and has_one :guest relationship in the corresponding serializer. Therefore, when the JSON data for appearance is returned, it will include the data for episode and guest.


The same can be used with many-to-many associations. Example: I want the JSON data to include guests along with data for episodes. Including the has_many :guests relationship in the corresponding episode serializer will return JSON data with the episode, and guests.


What helped me understand these concepts the most was seeing them in action. Practice coding different serializers to see the JSON data that is returned.
Items.Author: tartope

Items.Title: 方案：eqv? 对等？ | Scheme: eqv? vs equal?
Items.Description:
奥代·阿塔姆内

发表于照片由Piret Ilver在 Unsplash 上拍摄

您可以在 Stack Overflow 上阅读一些冗长的解释，但简而言之：

  • equal? 如果其参数具有相同的结构，则返回 true。 这与大多数语言中的==相当。
  • eqv? 是指针比较。 它查看内存地址。
    • 例外是数字。
    • 不像eq? 但是，它不会在int和float数据之间进行转换。

延伸阅读

  • 华盛顿大学页面



原文:

Odai Athamneh

Posted on May 6

Photo by Piret Ilver on Unsplash

You can read a few lengthy explanations on Stack Overflow, but in a nutshell:

  • equal? returns true if its arguments have the same structure. This is comparable to == in most languages.
  • eqv? is a pointer comparison. It looks at memory addresses.
    • The exception is numbers.
    • Unlike eq?, it will compare numeric data instead of addresses.
    • However, it won't convert between int and float data.

Further Reading

  • Stack Overflow question
  • Washington University page
Items.Author: heyodai

Items.Title: 2022 年 5 月 5 日：这周你学到了什么？ | May 5th, 2022: What did you learn this week?
Items.Description:
尼克·泰勒（他/他）

发表于又是一周中的那个时候。 如此出色的开发者，你这周学到了什么？ 它可能是编程技巧、职业建议等。

随意评论您学到的知识和/或参考您的 TIL 帖子以增加曝光度。



原文:
